title: Pleasant scent - "the tingle means it's working"
text:
This is a nice beard oil that smells masculine and clean. Here are the highlights:
- This is well packaged in a sturdy glass bottle with a glass tube and rubber bulb (all boxed in an attractive cardboard box); all pieces function well
- This "Lumberjack" scent is aptly-named; the cedar comes through, and it is reminiscent of an evergreen forest; very pleasant, and "bold" without being overwhelming
- The oil is not greasy and dissipates well, and aids in grooming the beard; the conditioning effects are adequate, and about average for a beard oil
- I use a variety of balms and oils, but I think I will be sticking with this one for the winter, as the season suits the scent
- Recommended for your cabinet collection

title: Love this product, really works in high humidity!
text: I found this easy to use and I really put it to the test, while on vacation in a high humidity area I used it on the top layer only, wasn't looking to go stick straight, more to calm the frizzy's on my outer layer & it worked like a charm. After a full day by the Ocean with incredible humidity, only slightly noticed any frizzy hair & I was really looking for it, will use this treatment again. The outer layer is very straight, and the under layers are still wavy, did not apply solution to under layers. Only time will tell how long it lasts, but so far very impressed. Did not notice any more fumes than at a salon, I do not have overly processed hair, hair actually looks darker and healthier, even my hubby commented. Just for info, I do color (dark brown), about every 4 weeks due to creeping gray's, but overall healthy hair, mid back length. Hope this helps others, great product and a fraction of the cost of a salon visit. (I think my bottle may last 4-5 treatments, considering I am only doing the top layers.

title: Dangerous product, unethical seller
text:
When I first saw this, I thought this would be the answer to my hair-drying prayers. Big mistake. The first time I attached this to my Conair dryer, it got so hot that it actually burned my skin. And I mean BURNED it. When my husband tried to remove it, it broke into pieces. A few days later, my hairdresser asked what had "fried" my hair. "If it burned your skin," she said, "imagine what it did to your hair."
Then the fun began with the vendor. I filed a request to return it, and they sent me 2 screens of legalese doubletalk about how they weren't the manufacturer, only the seller, so they weren't responsible, so they wouldn't refund my money. I mean, the word "Unbreakable" is in the name of the product - but when it broke during normal use, mayabeauty didn't see a problem with that! If this isn't a defective product, what is? Be aware that anything you buy from this seller is strictly 'buyer beware' - don't expect them to stand behind it. This product will burn you - and so will the seller.

title: WHY did they change the formula?It's an oily mess.
text: Why? Why? Why? I've been using this gentle cleanser for years - so many that I can't remember when I first used it. I've loyally used it through 3 name changes, packaging changes, and now new branding and packaging (why the same blue that that everyone else uses and a teeny-tiny logo that you can't read? Does not stand out on the shelf.) and a new formulation. But, with this new formulation, it is the end of the line for me. They made sunflower seed oil the most prominent ingredient besides water. It makes the cleanser oily - my face, hands, and washcloth are coated in sticky, oily residue mess that I can't get off. Gross. If I want oil on my skin, I will decide to put it there - and it would NEVER be sunflower seed oil. I don't add sunflower seed oil to my food - why would I want it all over my skin, my largest organ? It's just awful. I use several Earth Science products - I really like the brand. But this is not going to be one of them moving forward. So sad, it's like a bad break-up that I didn't see coming....

title: Decent shaver
text: I bought this to replace my older Norelco 965RX which can no longer hold charge. For the price, it is certainly look and feel a lot cheaper than my 10+ years 965RX. It shave about the same as the 965RX but lacks the battery life indicator, longer charge time, larger AC cord and no travel case. The saving grace for this model is that it has a low battery indicator unlike the newer Norelco 73xx or even the 7130/7140. The head is also all plastic with no adjustment (except for the steel blades and combs) unlike the all metal of the old. The head is definitely flexing more than the old but I am not sure if it really cut any closer than the old "Lift and Cut" blades. This shaver have both slots and foil on the shaver combs to get closer shave and I think it helps on short stubbles. It does have a nice feature that my old Norelco could not do: washable head. The trimmer is similar to the old which I think is much better than the version on the expensive Arcitec which is not sliding out at all to be useful.

title: Super Nice Set
text:
I’m a sucker for a good brush set. It’s even better when it comes with case and a sponge!

So the case is very sturdy and feels well made. You can use it as a brush holder and you can use it store brushes. I am using the shorter half as a brush holder and find that it blends in nicely with my other holders.

Now I’ll do a brief breakdown of the brushes and sponge.

The lip brush is great and it blends in my lipsticks really nice.

There are three brushes for eyes- eyeshadow brush, blending brush and angled liner. I have tried these and they are very soft and work very well.

The powder brush is a bit smaller than I would personally like for powder. It’s not quite dense but in a brush that is used for powder products that’s okay. When I’m buffing in the powder I don’t want the brush to be too dense or it will move the foundation around. I’ve been using this brush for blush because of it’s smaller shape and it’s working great.

Now the final two- the foundation brush and the sponge. I decided to do half of my foundation with the brush on one side, and the other half with the sponge on the other side.

The foundation brush is a typical brush of it’s kind. It did not make the foundation look streaky and applied very smooth.

The sponge is the most interesting piece in this set. It doesn’t feel super soft like a Beauty Blender or a Real Techniques sponge. It feels like a softer stress ball and feels a bit rubbery. I’ve never had a sponge like this. I soaked the sponge in water and drained the excess as I would do with any sponge. It felt heavier but did seem to soften a bit and did expand in size. I dabbed the foundation on my face and pressed the sponge around to press the product in and I got a really smooth finish. The sponge did not seem to absorb much product.

Overall I think it’s a very nice set!

I purchased this product at a discount in exchange for an honest review. I did not get paid to do this review and my opinions are 100% honest.

title: Works Great!
text:
I used the Via Cosmedics Anti-Aging Peptide MegaMud the day after I used the Perfect Image Pre-Peel Salicylic Deep Gel Exfoliating Cleanser - I say this because the cleanser shrank my pores considerably; the amount I usually get from a mud mask, so when I used this mud mask I wasn't expecting to get anymore results, but it managed to make my pores look even smaller. That is one of the benefits, and reasons I use mud masks, smaller pores! If you have those cavernous pores on your cheeks like me, then this will help.

As for the MegaMud mask, it has a strong Metholatum smell to it. I mean, Mentholatum would stand up and say, "Daaaaam!". However, I didn't smell it after I had it on my face. I guess that sense packed up and left or blocked it out.

The mask is a thin, creamy consistency that spreads super easy with great coverage, so you don't need a lot to completely cover your face. It also makes it easy to rinse off. Some mud masks it seems like you need a chisel to remove, but not this one. Not only did it improve the appearance of my pores, but it made my skin feel smooth and soft.

I am using a micro needle roller at .25mm on my scalp for hair growth, or at least hopeful hair growth.

This roller has fewer needles than my other, so I decided to increase the number of stroke repetitions to account for the reduced number of needles, no big deal.

First thing I noticed. The packaging is nice and sterile.

The quality of the handle is better than anything else I've tried. I think it's some sort of metal. It feels really good in my hand not having plastic.

When using it, it rolls controllably. Very nice feeling of control.

The micro needles seem to be the same length as my other .25mm, so that's accurate.

Overall, this is a high quality product. I like it better than my higher needle count head because the handle is nicer.

I'll see if I actually grow any hair, but the product seems really good, I'd recommend it.

title: Small Angled Blending Brush This is a good set of brushes for someone just getting into makeup
text:
This set contains the following brushes:
1. Round Blending Brush
2. Angled Blending Brush
3. Flat Blending Brush
4. Tapered Contouring Brush
5. Angled Contouring Brush
6. Small Tapered Contouring Brush
7. Small Flat Blending Brush
8. Small Round Blending Brush
9. Small Angled Contouring Brush
10. Small Angled Blending Brush
This is a good set of brushes for someone just getting into makeup. The larger brushes are intended for face/contour, but not blush; the smaller brushes seem intended for eye contour and blending. The brushes are very soft; however, the ferrules are not as full as they could be, leading to somewhat floppy brush heads, which means less control of the brush and product. The smaller brushes should have lower ferrules so the use has more control over where color or product is laid.

The brush set comes with a plastic coated brush roll which needed to be aired out upon arrival. Once the odor was gone, it proves to be a pretty durable case for toting the brushes around, with individual pockets for each brush and a protective flap that folds over. Since it is coated in plastic, it wipes clean easily. The design is a little gaudy, but, each to their own, right?

I received this product at a discount in exchange for an honest review.

title: Different colors to fit just about every color out there. Powder blends still and gives a softer look than foundation and thick
text:
This contouring kit is so much fun! I've been playing with contouring a little over the past few months but was trying to piece everything together with random finds at the stores and as a beginner it was hard to pick the correct colors I need to do a true contoured look. Having this palette with so many choices makes it easy for me to play and perfect my look. The powder goes on well and the colors transfer great onto skin. Using powder instead of a heavier concealer or foundation gives it a Much softer look and not like I've caked a bunch of crap onto my face. They blend together well and last all day. They include an ebook tutorial on contouring tips and tricks in an email after purchase and I found it to be extremely helpful. There are also about a billion and one videos in YouTube that show you clearly step by step. I am much more confident in my makeup application now and can't believe how easy it is with the right tools. There is a good amount of powder of each color and I think this palette will last me a long time. There are many colors to fit just about every skin tone and color, a fantastic starter kit! I love it!

I did receive my contouring makeup palette free or at a promotional price for testing and evaluation purposes. I was not paid for my review and the was asked only to provide whatever feedback I may have regarding the product, whether it be positive or negative. My review is solely based on my experience and honest opinions of this palette.

I found that my mascara, regular mascara, does a better job of lengthening my lashes than this set did.
This purchase I do regret. It was a real waste of money for me. How many tubes of good ole mascara I could of purchased in
it's place..

It seemed to string along. The endless mascara string, never seemed to amaze. It would string for quite a distance.
Then we come to the fibers. After reading the instructions a few times, an still the fibers end up leaving my lashes.
An where I dray the line is. I can't walk through my day with foreign fibers floating within my eyes. It's like standing with your eye's wide open in the middle of a dirt storm.. OUCH!
Maybe this works for some folks. Though it didn't do it for me. Sorry

title: This product amazingly, works!
text:
In combination with an appropriate diet and healthy life, this product seems to work pretty well. I've noticed changes in the aspect of my belly skin which indeed is firmer and toner. I also use this product on my thighs which had that "orange skin" aspect. With repetitive massages by "rolling the skin" morning and night every day, my thighs are smoother and the cellulite has diminished.This product has a pleasant smell and is not greasy at all.

I am a slim person, but have accumulated some cellulite after my pregnancies so I think the product is perfect for women like me, who just need a little help to be tone again. But for those who have real bad cellulite problems, I believe that this wouldn't be the miracle solution that they are looking for.

It's definitively a keeper for me!

I really do like the scent though. It’s a little bit citrusy and very light. The other bottle of stuff has a slightly spicy scent to it but it’s not overpowering. I will say that there were no English directions included with this, so I had to end up going online to find out whether or not I was supposed to wash out the other bottle and you are in fact, supposed to rinse it out after you put it in and let it sit for six seconds. Neither of these felt particularly oily to me, so I was a little disappointed with how greasy my hair felt after I dried it. I’m not sure if you’re using a blow dryer made a difference or not. title: NOT HUMAN HAIR
text: Okay, so I figured I'd give these a try as I usually spend way too much money on high quality tape in extensions. When I saw these I thought they'd be a potential cheaper alternative. When I received the hair it looked good, very thin, but the hair was soft and felt great. After application, my hair looked and felt great (my natural hair is about shoulder length, so all of the hair below my shoulders is only the extensions). I got them trimmed, layered, etc, to blend my hair. A few days after installation I decided that I wanted to curl my hair. Well, once I put that curler to the extensions on LOW HEAT, I heard the horrific "sizzling" noise and smelled a burning plastic-y smell. I instantly pull my hair out of the iron and the hair was literally fried, as synthetic hair does when heat is put onto it. At most, this hair is partially synthetic. If you look closely you can even see the synthetic hairs. They're super shiny (not a healthy human hair shine, but a plastic shine. Think of the shine or reflection on a water bottle). If I had known these weren't 100% human hair I would not have ordered them. Not only do the extensions basically get ruined after putting heat on them but they tangle easily, and just always look fake too shiny, and the hair clumps together, which I do in fact blame on the heat. If you're in the market for short term hair extensions that you can not put heat on then these are perfect for you. If not, you've got to invest in something more expensive that actually IS 100% human hair.

I was getting a 1oz bottle for about $14 and using a tiny drop at a time to conserve it. When I found this product I was thrilled! $20 for a 4oz bottle of 100%, cold pressed, unrefined argan oil!? title: Perfect for my curly hair!
text:
A silk pillowcase isn't enough when you live in a dry climate with curly hair that is prone to frizz. It's exposed to the elements, with minimal protection. Regardless of the product you use, there are still a million other things a woman must contend with such as hormones, aging, etc., that don't help. I notice a huge difference with my hair when sleeping with a satin cap.

I definitely recommend for medium-thick, mid-length hair. After trying several caps, this is the best quality thus far. I love the size, and after several washes, the material is intact, and the caps still feel silky. I wash these in either the hand wash or delicate cycle in cold water in my machine. Hang dry.

title: Great at washing brushes, ok for drying
text: This product is great as an all in one to wash brushes and dry them. I used to always have soap left in my brush, but the refining section ensures that there is no soap left after a few seconds of scrubbing. I like how my brushes just stand up to dry after I’m finished with washing, and it is space saving. Like others have mentioned, it does take a few tries to load the brushes in there correctly and they will fall out a few times. One downfall with this product is the fact that the smaller brushes in the middle never really dry properly. I washed my brushes 3 days ago, had the product with my brushes sitting on a dish drying mat, and most of the brushes inside the small brush holding part are still quite wet. Some larger brushes will not fit in this product. Also DO NOT wash your beauty blender on this. This product rips up my beauty blender so that there are chunks of foam missing. I don’t regret my purchase, in fact I am quite happy that it dries my larger brushes effectively and thoroughly helps to wash my makeup brushes. If you’re thinking about it, buy it. Just maybe don’t use the inside brush holder, dry your tiny brushes elsewhere.

title: Love it - Favorite One - Right now
text:
This is my favorite body scrub. The sugar chamomile one. I have tried a lot of different ones. One years ago that is no longer on the market was my absolute favorite, which is why this only gets 4 stars. I did get a bad batch recently, which I attribute to either sitting too long in some warehouse or supply chain issues. It has been a little tough to find.

I have very sensitive skin & even worse allergies & this does not break me out or set off my allergies. When properly mixed & emulsified, it’s great. It works on my skin the way I want it to. It doesn’t dry my skin out or make it sticky, oily or itchy/flakey. It also doesn’t destroy my shower/tub. It washes politely & completely down the drain & doesn’t stain or send grit everywhere. There is no remaining residue on my skin or my surroundings.

I don’t love the smell. It’s a mostly pleasant natural smell, but it took some getting used to. In my opinion, it’s way better than the other 2 products this company makes in this category. They reek to high heaven & make a terrible mess. I still regret using the pink-salt one, the perfume was too strong & awful. The grit was too strong & stained the silicone sealant on my tub. It’s still there.

I like this one for all the previously stated reasons, but I don’t love the smell or the tube. The seal on all of there products is strong, which is great for assurances, safety & transport, but it’s also tiny & a bitch to get off in the shower. So, don’t forget to take it off 1st, the way I sometimes do. I usually have to use my teeth & you know how that goes… yeah. The bottle could be a little bigger too. I don’t know what sizing the product would cost, but it would be nice for the consumer. Also, the last inch or 2 of product, in the tube doesn’t always come out. Again it’s the tube’s design & they all work that way. It’s VERY annoying. If they would make the inner nozzle/outlet a little bigger & the “top” a steeper pitch, it would be 100% better. Work with gravity & let me use all the product I pay for please (especially at these prices)! Thanks!

Anyway, this is currently my go to scrub. I don’t usually use it on my face, but I have been considering trying it on my scalp, it seems gentle enough. And it dissolves enough. I will add to this review if I do. I have been able to find this product at Sprouts, grocery store as well as on Amazon. I think I have seen it at Natural Grocers & Whole Foods too, but it’s been since before COVID, so not sure. I have never seen a sample product, but if you can find them, I would suggest trying all of them that way - if that works for your needs - before you buy. Depending on quality, which has been wonky the last 3 tubes, I get between 3-9 showers out of 1 tube. The better blended it is, the closer to 6 showers it is. I tend to use a lot. I am generous & I use it on most of my body. I do really like this stuff & can say I would recommend it to someone I know, whose skin care I know about, given the opportunity. It is a natural product, if you are allergic to chamomile tea, do not use this product, ever. If not, it’s, in my opinion, one of the nicest body scrub products out there. I like a lot of Desert Essence products.

title: Best I've tried (and an apology)
text:
I'm sorry folks - every time I decide I have a favorite hair care product, whether shampoo, conditioner, or a styling product, the manufacturer discontinues it within a year. And because I discovered I like this stuff last year, it has apparently been discontinued. It was about $4 previously, and easily available form Walmart and Amazon. Prices have tripled online, and Walmart no longer stocks it.

That's too bad, because this stuff is close to perfect. I like the hold - it lasts, without being stiff or sticky, the easy application, and the fact that it leaves you with a natural matte finish and doesn't look slick or greasy. At $4 it was an incredible value, but now you might be able to find something better. I don't know what try next, because I've never been a fan of paying $10-$15 for higher-end brands (didn't like the ones I tried), and I'm not counting on this being available here as what is becoming old stock. There are other Got2B products in similar jars but they are nothing like this clay.

title: If used correctly, it will scrub your back
text:
I'm reviewing the back scrubber, not the ups guy, not the plastic it was shipped in, not the flavor of the scrubber, and not any other thing associated with the overall logistical process of the product making it to my front door.

Great, scrubs as it should. Couldn't be happier, description is accurate. I expected to receive a back scrubber that could reach the middle of my back. It is as it should be.
If you are looking for a rocket ship or the Swiss army knife version of a Loofah, do not buy this product. If you have a hard time reaching spots on your back, during bathing and personal grooming, buy this. It will help.
I don't care about the packaging or how pretty it is, it arrived undamaged and had not been used by anyone before me. At least, I'm telling myself that no one before me used it. I'm not sure how they test the product prior to shipping. I did wash it before use.
So, in summary:
It scrubs the back well, there is a section that is a little rougher in texture than the rest of the Loofah.
It is not a rocket ship.
It doesn't matter about frivolous things such as packaging color or texture of the plastic it was shipped in. It's a back scrubber, not a living room conversational piece.

title: I really really like the DAELONG products
text:
I have been using the serum for a little over 3 weeks. I really really like the DAELONG products. Take a look at my other review of the hair treatment. It’s absolutely amazing. However, the quick grow serum didn’t work for me. Its consistency was too watery for my taste. You can definitely tell it has argan oil in it. There is something else in this product that causes it to be a little soapy if you apply more than 3 squirts.

I usually apply this product to freshly washed, but dry hair. Just to allow it to have its full effect. I also applied it nightly and something during the day if I remembered before work. I did not lose any more hair while using this product but I also didn’t gain any hair as well. I wish I would’ve taken some pictures to really be sure that I haven’t had hair growth but I watch for growth every day. I can guarantee I am pretty sure. I like products with a thicker consistency so what works for me may not work for you. I have very thick no porosity hair.

I received this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

title: Great for maintaining those guitar pickin' fingernails
text: OK - so I'm a fingerstyle guitar player (James Taylor / Chet Atkins wannabe). Fingernail care is an annoying part of the business, I'm afraid. And unfortunately, I have a nail that splits often. Silk reinforcements with resin and activator has been a lifesaver for maintaining nails, and this sanding block is part of the process. I use a diamond file to keep the fingernail ends shaped and the sanding block makes the finished ends smooth and snag free. Snagging is NOT good for finger picking guitar! I'm still learning to use it, but it also does a great job of smoothing the nail before applying silk and resin on the split nail and Sally Hanson's triple hard titanium polish on the rest of my nails. Yeah, I get the other guys ribbing me over my shiny nails - which is pretty obvious when they see both hands together - only the right hand gets the fussing. But then they hear me play and the ribbing stops. My only complaint about this would be useful life - it seems to wear down rather quickly. But I'm probably not being fair - that diamond nail file lasts forever - can't expect a soft foam sanding block to last that long.

If you want a product that really keeps your hair-style through the whole day, I highly recommend this; however, be prepared to scrub your head hard and use a lot of shampoo/conditioner. Aside from that, I'll be sticking to Krieger's regular soft hold gel.

title: Perfect trio of products for clean and healthy skin.
text:
This combo is heaven! The foaming face wash is light and gentle, and is equally effective in both summer and winter months. I have sensitive skin that develops dry patches, itchy rashes, (or both at once) if I use a product with remotely harsh ingredients. The foam cleanser spreads generously, so it lasts for quite a long time, and it rinses off very easily, reducing the need for unnecessary scrubbing and rubbing. For the last 10 years since I discovered this line, Caudalie has never caused such reactions. The scent is also quite pleasant.

The serum really helps restore elasticity and mousture with ZERO oily feel on the skin. It is one of the very few products labeled "serum" that I find perfectly suitable to use morning and night, before another moisturizer (or without), and under primer and makeup. Again, it's perfect for sensitive skin. It's not lying about helping replenish moisture. It's especially great in the colder months to counter the dry air and constant temperature changes.

The night cream is absolutely fantastic, even without the serum (but using them together is basically a guarantee that you'll wake up with healthy- looking and healthy- feeling, clean skin). It spreads well, so a little goes a long way, absorbs quickly but isn't "disappeared" to the point your face gets dry in the night, and is not cakey or oily (you won't leave a lotion stain on your pillowcase). I HIGHLY recommend this trio. The price is fair and matches what you'd pay at the Caudalie.US store or Sephora.

title: Great Toothbrush, Especially for College
text:
I've used this toothbrush, the essence 5600, and the flexcare r910. The flexcare is more powerful and has better brush heads (it also avoids the problem of having water trapped inside the brush and growing mold), but it's also far more expensive. The essence 5600 is a waste of money in my opinion as it has the same performance as this toothbrush, but suffers from a shoddy li-on battery that dies after about a year or so of use. This avoids the battery problem by using replaceable AA batteries and lasts quite a while on each replacement.

The only major con is with the brush head design. There is a a gap between the brush body and head, and this gap tends to fill with water after use. This unfortunately leads to black mold growing in the cavity. It's easy to avoid this by simply unscrewing the head after brushing and rinsing out the cavity, but it's an annoyance nonetheless.

All in all, this is a great cheap way to get the benefits of sonicare.

title: GREAT toothbrush!
text:
This is a great toothbrush! I have gingivitis and after only ONE use I could see improvement. In addition, it took off every bit of plaque that I could see, i.e. I thought my front bottom gums were really clean and when toothbrush was done, I saw that they weren't as clean as could they could have been.

I hate to floss ("I don't have time right now!") and this does it for me.

In regards to the timer, I had no idea how long two minutes was! It sounds like nothing, and yet I waited and waited for the timer - I thought it was broken but it wasn't; it showed me how me I DON'T take to brush my teeth. Great feature.

The one thing that is kind of hard to get used to is that you just run the toothbrush over your teeth/gums/tongue and I (quite naturally) am used to the back and forth way of brushing and I kept finding myself having to stop working and let the toothbrush do its work.

To sum up, for anyone wanting to get their teeth REALLY clean and not have to put a lot of maintenance into it, you should really think about investing in this toothbrush. It's a really great toothbrush! I KNOW my dentist is going to be happy.

title: I have fine, wavy shoulder-length hair
text:
I have fine, wavy shoulder-length hair. Likes to get especially tangly around the back. Anyone else in this camp knows that finding products/brushes/styles for this type can be borderline excruciating, but this brush works very well!

Pros:
The "naturally conditions oil" claim seems to be a valid one, i do feel that i need to condition less frequently and oils are more evenly distributed from root to end using this product
Experiencing significantly less tearing, snarls and general despair than when brushing with other types
My hair is shiny and smooth, and I have to spend less time styling if i use this!

Cons:
Has less "grab" than non-bristle brushes; I often have to comb first or separate sections more than i would have to with a standard brush with longer "teeth." That being said, the end result is worth it!

title: The Razor that cuts away the competition
text: It is such a simple idea. Take a manual razor and an electric razor and create a hybrid electrical manual razor at a cheap price. Forget about buying any kind of electrical razor or manual razor again. This thing works using a single AAA battery (and is worth investing in some rechargable AAA batteries) which slips into the base by pulling out the end piece. Just press the green button and the razor starts to vibrate. This vibration stimulates the skin and gets those hairs to prick up a little bit so that razor blades (all three baldes, i.e - Mach III/M3) catch better creating a cleaner smoother cut. To be honest the old Mach III model was absolutely great but the M3 Power razor just upgrades that razor into a totally new device. There is simply nothing on the market that remotely resembles this kind of product... nothing. I would not hesitate in sending anyone down to the local drug store with a $20 to pick up this wonderful product that... quite frankly... just making shaving enjoyable again!

text:

I like to give it about one minute to get warmed up prior to using it, so I just plug it in and turn it on while I'm pre-combing my beard to get any major tangles out.

The only reason that I didn't give this product five stars is because the swivel feature on the power cord seems to need a little assistance from me in swiveling it down and out of the way. title: Where the heck is your quality control?
text:
Look, I know I wasn't paying for the best device in the world at this price point. But when it comes to something as basic as converting a 9V battery to a direct current you would think they would be able to solder a basic board.

Look at these photos, this is all that is inside. It requires very basic soldering, and yet they somehow dripped solder right on the board, which shorts it, and said "Yeah screw it, send it, I'm getting paid anyway, I'll deal with the RMA later."

Such a shame, you'd think with such a easy job they'd be able to do it properly.

I'll be resoldering this myself, but the majority of people buying this won't be able to do this.

title: Great product
text:
I use this product nearly on a daily basis, and I really like it. If used correctly, the amount of product in this tube will last for months. The operative words are "used correctly." Too many women try to apply foundation, of any kind, with their fingers. Thats never a good idea, because it will always lead to the streaky, too dark appearance mentioned in the last review. Also, since you are using too much product that way, the tube will be empty far sooner than is should be.
The best way to apply foundation is with a kabuki style make up brush. The long handled one with the small, soft head is best for applying all skin foundations. Apply two or three drops to the back of your hand, dip the brush in as needed and swirl onto your face. Do this and I guarantee that your Botanilift foundation will give you that air brushed look of naked skin. That being said, I still recommend the use of a light weight sunblock under this or any other product, regardless of whether or not it has an SPF. A girl who wants to ensure the long term health and appearance of her skin can never have too much sunblock! I hope this helps others who are having difficulty with this and other foundation products.

title: It works.
text:
Really well. I was surprised that it got all the coarse hair from my nether regions. And inner thighs. It doesn't get completely baby butt bald though. My first attempt, I had hair over half inch long. The area under my tummy where the happy trail is, was the area that didn't get thoroughly smooth. The rest, like the um.. sack was smooth and hairless. First run. I was impressed. So I ran a second attempt to get the happy trail area smooth and more hairless. It only got marginally smoother. And I burned my inner thigh a bit.

It's pretty easy to use. Just cake on, hang out for 15 minutes. And hop in the shower. Put the glove on and just wipe away. The effects last about 2 weeks or so. Then I did it again. once. And it got the whole area pretty smooth. Again, the happy trail area was just... not as smooth. But for the record I have super thick hair down there.

I totally recommend this product. The whole kit works like a charm.

title: Magic Serum!!
text:
To me retinol is a magic serum!! It's great for anti aging. Also acne! Retinol can unclog pores. Also reduce their appearance. After a few uses. My friends were asking if I changed my makeup. They said my skin looked so smooth!! Later in the week my kids asked if I took a nap. They said I looked well rested. The retinol helped my makeup from not settling in the fine lines. My makeup also seemed to have lasted longer. I didn't need as many touch ups!
The thing about retinol is that it can be very drying. It's suggested to follow retinol with a OZ Naturals moisturizer. I use 'Sea Infused Herbal Moisturizer ' by OZ Naturals. It helped tremendously. I don't have any red or dry patches. Also only use this at night. The retinol and the sun could cause a bad reaction. It was very easy to put this product as part of my nighttime ritual.

title: Took a chance and got soft, full hair with movement
text: This shampoo was recommended to me by my hair stylist for my thinning hair. She said it might help with growth and can help make your hair shiny. I have read good and bad things about it, especially that it makes your hair dry. But after shampooing with it for 5 days now, I have to say I love this shampoo! My hair does feel somewhat dry after shampooing, but my hair feels that way with most volumizing shampoos. I always use conditioner after shampooing since my hair is also dry. I used Mane & Tail's moisturizing/texturizing conditioner with this, and the results are absolutely wonderful. I tried a different brand conditioner with it, and my hair still felt good but not as good as with the Mane & Tail conditioner. After using M&T shampoo and conditioner, my hair feels soft, full and like it moves, rather than being stiff. I have tried countless shampoos, cheap and expensive, and none have made my hair feel this way! My husband agrees that my hair is softer, and we both think it looks fuller and fluffier. I'm going on vacation soon, and I don't want to be without it. I don't know if it will help my hair grow -- too early to tell, but if it does, I will be sure to update my post.

title: Weak sticks
text: These cotton swabs have pushed the limit of lowering quality beyond what I can accept.  The length of these swabs is 1/8" shorter than the traditional size, though it's got about the same amount of cotton as most Chinese swabs.  There's still about four out of a hundred that will snap due to wood grain weakness, but I understand that's inherent in the wood, itself, as wood is a natural product and has inconsistencies that you need to accept if you're going to use the product.  The problem with these swabs is that they come on a wooden stick the thickness of which is a whole, new low.  I've been using cotton swabs on wooden sticks for three decades and I've never before met wooden sticks this thin and weak before.  With these swabs you've got a high risk of breakage even with the sticks that don't have wood grain issues simply because of how thin and weak they've made the sticks.  If you only intend to use swabs for extremely light pressure work these are fine, but they can't handle the pressure of normal or average tasks.  They've skimpped on quality a little too much for me to recommend this product.  I would not buy them again.

title: Shaves as good as a blade - if you use it right
text:
This is the second 7775X I have owned. The first lasted two years, but this after extensive travel all over the world. It does shave as well as a blade (I use one of those too now and then) if you use it right (see below)

Positives

1) Quick charging - less than an hour to charge.
2) Long lasting - I have used it 10 days without recharging - great if you travel.
3) Seems to prevent ingrown hairs - which is something I run into when I use a blade consistently. My face definitely breaks out much less when I use this razor versus a blade (and I own some expensive blade razors as backup!).
4) So long as you follow instructions (don't press down hard, but when you need to go over an area again, do it firmly) you won't get nicked. Nice when you are in a strange hotel in a strange time zone and waking up jet lagged but needing to look presentable for a meeting.

Negatives

1) A little expensive
2) The shaving lotion cartridges are also expensive - I would just buy a can of regular shaving Gel (eg Edge).
3) Constantly pressing the dispenser for more gel can be a pain in the neck, and you go through those cartridges quickly (one every 10 days at most). If I wanted to redesign this shaver, I would junk the shaving dispensor entirely - I now find it just another moving part with the hinged lid.
4) I wish Norelco had come up with a way for this razor to use a standard charger jack of some kind, as having another electrical cord when I travel (especially one that does not work with multiple corded adaptors such as I-go) means I don't take the cord unless I am gone more than 8-10 days.

To use this right, just buy regular shaving gel, put it on your face, wait 30 seconds for the gel to soften your beard, and go for it. This is the best way I have found to get a clean, smooth shave. Gel in a can also has the advantage of going on thickly (versus the Coolskin dispensor) and not drying up quickly. Also, having a can of the shaving gel when you travel (checked baggage of course) can also come in handy if your Norelco runs out of charge, and you need to switch to that backup Gillette you should have in your shaving kit (I travel with enough cords as it is).

title: Absolutely love this oil!!!!
text:
This Kardashian dry black seed oil is by far the best shining/straightening oil I have ever used in my life. I am on 2 different types of chemo drugs and it has played havoc on my long hair. Kim Kardashian knows her stuff for sure! I have tried at least 30 different products to help with the frizz in my hair. I received a small sample bottle of this in a monthly subscription box I get. A small bottle of this lasted me about 4 months of daily use. I used it right after a wash while my hair was still wet and then right after blow drying and using the straightening iron and then a tiny bit to give my hair that shiny finished, sleek look. Let me tell you, my hair looks and feels like silk after using this. First off, the tangles fall right out. The heat from my iron doesn't damage my hair any longer and my husband says my hair feels like spun silk! This is also a dry oil so there is no oily build up or feel at all. Also, look at the AMAZING price that this is offered at! Wow! I will be heartbroken if they ever stop selling this stuff!
Now, there is only one con to this dry black seed oil. It has a small aroma that to me smells like pencil shavings. Almost like when you were in elementary school standing at the pencil sharpener getting your pencils all sharpened up for your tests! The good news is that the smell doesn't stay in your hair but for a few minutes. Other than that, you can't go wrong with this product!

title: What a messy disappointment!
text:
I had LOW hopes for this to begin with, so I can't say I'm surprised, but it would have been nice if it would have worked at all. I saw a review that said it was more like a gel or cream that just sat on your hair, which is about accurate. That's why I went ahead and tried it out...I wasn't looking for a permanent hair color, just a fun color changing experiment.
I do have darker hair so it was hard to tell that the purple was in my hair and upon drying it like the directions say it did turn to a turquoise color, but only in specks. Imagine if you applied a cream/gel type product, then blow dried your hair and it turned to a crayon type texture.
THEN you bush your hair after taking forever to blow dry, & There you have it! NOW there's crayon type dust all over your legs and floor!! My hair had a turquoise tint to the top of my head where I applied it (there was definitely not enough product for my whole head. Only three small sections on my top layer of hair) and no where else, so I decided to straighten it...what happens when you try to straighten a crayon? THAT'S right! Melty turquoise on my straightener! I never saw the purple again. IDK if I did something wrong by following the directions to a T, or maybe it doesn't work well with thick, coarse hair types. Either way, this was definitely not what I saw advertised. I'm not necessarily upset about it, I guess I had fun testing it out and making a mess lol. I would have been ok with the waste of time and money if the color change would have actually worked, but oh well, now I know.

title: Very good soap
text:
I would call this a very good soap. Not quite excellent, due to a couple of peeves, but very close. No 5 Star, but 9 out of 10.

The scent is quite enjoyable and is as labeled, a bay rum and orange. While it is not overpowering; I kind of would have preferred the orange to be a bit toned back to let more of the bay rum through. But I would count that more of a personal preference.

It works more or less as one would expect for cleaning. I found that it formed a light lather with just hand rubbing and would need a washcloth to build a thicker foam. After cleaning, it would rinse off easily with no leftover films or feelings other than just clean.

This soap is also very good for a wet shave. While I am relatively new to wet shaving and only have used a puck of Col Conk to compare to. Once that puck of Col Conk was gone, my bar of this soap was just about done and I tossed the last sliver into my bowl. This soap lathers up beautifully thick with a badger hair brush, and then after application, would stay foamed on the face for the full shave. If anything, I would say this soap is superior to the Col Conk for shaving, and the Conk was a good product and is highly rated.
Heck. If Frannie sold this soap in a round cake suitable for a shave bowl, I would probably stick with it for shaving.

title: Excellent value!
text:
When I bought a bottle of SunLabs self tanner to try, I decided to upgrade my mitt too!

Previously, I was using a cheap ULTA brand one that I picked up when I first got into self tanning. This tanning mitt from SunLabs is seriously a step above, and for around the same price!

I like that it is black, so it doesn’t show staining like my ULTA one, and it has a fine but dense velvety texture, not a sponge-y one, so things go on so smooth and streak-free without the mitt soaking up all the product.

I also appreciate that it’s shaped more like a glove, so it doesn’t just slide around when I rub, and that it’s lined! Under my ULTA mitt, I had to wear a latex glove b/c product would seep through so badly my palms would stain. I haven’t had that problem at all with this mitt.

Overall, I would def recommend buying this mitt, or picking up one of the SunLabs bundles where it’s included.

title: The best I've had yet
text:
I've been using electric shavers of one form or another for over fifty years, and this one I rate as the best yet.

First it's one of the most comfortable. I can comfortably shave the back of my neck with this shaver where others irritated. Second, it's by far the fastest shaver yet, plus it gets any long hairs in its path. I attribute at least part of its speed to the third row of cutters which sets this one apart from many others. Third, it works fine wet, lotion or water, or dry. I'm using up some of the lotion cartridges from a previous Norelco dispensing shaver and it works very smoothly with them. Fourth, it went well over a month on the initial charge. Fifth, the built in trimmer is effective and convenient as opposed to some trimmers I've had that had to be attached in place of the shaving head. And finally it's easy to clean and packs light and compact for traveling.

The battery level meter is nice for checking when the charge is full. When the battery does finally give up and you're in the middle of a shave the charging cord can be used directly into the shaver to finish, then when ready for charging it can be connected to the charging stand for a regular charge. Charging is done relatively fast also, in roughly about an hour or so it's full and ready to go another month or longer.

There's often the argument between basic types of shavers, foil or rotary being two of the subjects. I recently tried one of the newer and recommended foil shavers and it's now on the shelf. Foil shavers for me miss too much, long hairs for sure, but they are not as effective on the regular stubble for me as a rotary shaver. That would be bad enough but my experience with foil shavers was if you missed a place in the previous shave, you won't get it next time either even though you go over it. I often had to use the trimmers on foil shavers, even the best of them, for just regular shaving chores.

I've bought and used a lot of electric shavers, but I've never had one this good before of any brand.

title: Time Travel Into A Quality Shave
text: For 340 years, men have used the straight razor as a principal means of manual shaving. I’ve used one for 14 years employing replaceable blades but remember the feeling of a barber shave with a permanent blade straight razor that had been properly stropped; the kind of blade used since 1680 to separate a man from his facial hair.  I’ve yearned for the vintage experience of stropping my own stainless steel blade and applying metal to beard.  Today, I received this handmade straight razor, stropped it just a bit, and had a longed for experience of truly vintage shaving that left my face smooth as a river rock.  I’m elated!  So many reviews are written by folks with redwood sized chips on their shoulders. I ignored them and ordered this razor anyway. I could not be more pleased with this purchase. It was shave ready.  My stropping exercise was more just to have the full experience.  One review said there was no room for his finger between the handle and blade. I have large hands and, when the razor is held correctly, there is ample room for my finger. The weight and balance is excellent and it is simple to stabilize it for a close yet lacerationless shave.  This razor gets five stars across the board with the exception of the ‘for beginners’ category.  HELLOOO!  STRAIGHT RAZOR!!!  This is a serious piece of sharpened 440 stainless steel!  The only good straight razor for beginners is made of rubber!! If you’re ready for a vintage shaving experience at a great price, you’ve arrived!

title: Misleading Photos/Descriptions
text:
Much like another reviewer, I also received two 30ct. bags of wipes instead of the pictured single 60ct. bag. The wipes appear to be the same and work as intended, but it's still NOT what I wanted and NOT what was ostensibly being offered. Moreover, this is not the first time this happened. I chalked the first time up to a fluke/shipping error, but after receiving another shipment of mini-bags, that clearly is NOT the case. The 30ct. bags are what are being sold. This pictures, descriptions, and even the product labels on the bag are misleading.

To the seller, if you're selling in good faith then please change the pictures and descriptions to accurately reflect what's being sold - 2 mini-bags of wipes instead of 1 normal-sized one.

The only thing missing in my opinion is a travel case. I also have the rechargeable version although I haven't checked to see if the case that came with that will work with this unit.

The replacement brush heads for the rechargeable unit work interchangeably with this one so that's a bonus. I did take note of what other reviewers said about the battery compartment allowing water to leak in so each time I change batteries I take a cheap lip balm and rub it over the rubber o-ring for a better seal.

title: Great set...All you need in one bundle. Great gift for someone.
text:
I am a fan of MARLOW products...I have several others not included in this set and all are great. I'll break down each product briefly below.

BAG: Very nice, basic black but well padded and holds it's shape. I basic in shape but I higher quality then usual.

SOAP: Great balsam/fir scent. Excellent balance of exfoliation and suds. Leaves body invigorated, soft and smelling great!

SHAVE: Has a tea scent, hint of some sort of alcohol. Builds into a creamy texture, not much sud but decent coverage. Does it's job. Finishes with a clean scent.

FACE WASH: Nice balsam/fir scent to it. A little goes a long way! Very slick, seems to not penetrate but wash the exterior of skin. Takes some work to wash off. Finishes with a green tea scent and refreshed.

FACE MOISTURIZER: Has a tea/alcohol scent at first...Goes on smooth. Dries fairly quickly, leaving a sweet green tea scent. Leaves face feeling soft not oily.

POMADE: Great scent, more cologne like. Goes on easy, not too tacky or slick. Does a decent job.

LIP BALM: Awesome balm!!! At first I thought it was an odd addition to the set. But man I love this balm. Goes on with a hint of sweet tea. Not waxy, not glossy, just a perfect balance that keeps lips moisturized.

title: Nice wig!
text:
I purchased this wig in black for a Halloween costume for work. I was planning on dressing up as Mia Wallace from Pulp Fiction. I had purchased a five dollar black wig from Walmart and it looked awful and had to be returned!! I decided I would give this a go, but at this price I didn't expect much. Boy, was I wrong!

This wig is a great quality! The product picture is an accurate representation of the actual product. The hair looks real and doesn't scream "fake". It held almost it's perfect shape right out of the bag. Couple of shakes and it was fine. It came with a hair net which was nice. The hair on the wig is very thick.No thin spots like you would find on cheap Halloween wigs. The bangs were not too long on me (I read from other reviewers they were too long and needed to be trimmed). However, the front pieces meant to scoop under the chin just came straight out and were kind of funny looking. They could have been easily adjusted with a little trimming.

In the end I decided not to wear the costume. This was in no way the wigs fault. No matter how much makeup I put on I looked absolutely awful in jet black hair. I'm about as fair a complexion as you can get, so I looked almost sickly in this color. However, at this price I might by a wig or two for fun in different, more complimenting colors in the future.

title: Overly drying
text:
I received this product complimentary from Influenster and Yardley in exchange for my honest opinion.

I have used Yardley London bar soaps before; but it had been years, so I was looking forward to trying again. I always see them in my local dollar store and think they smell nice, but couldn't remember why I didn't buy them again.

Now I do.

Heavily fragranced, overly drying, and has added dyes. I do like that they are SOMEWHAT natural and that the packaging is earth friendly, but just cannot justify even spending a dollar on it when there are so many other great options for the same price. They feel almost misleading with their approach, like they want to appear more animal and earth friendly than they actually are. Product just doesn't match advertising.

The box states it also exfoliates, but I could not feel any exfoliating properties whatsoever. Left my skin extremely dry and flakey all over. This is a hard no for me.

However, with only a few hairs causing me to be embarrassed, I felt I needed to invest in this equipment. Also, when I pluck these few hairs, I run the risk of a pimple. I found an excellent video on YouTube which helped me to prepare how to use this equipment. The first time I did my facial hairs, I placed it on number three and began shaky hands. It worked! I'm sure in time; I will see improvement along with my hands not so shaky.

Update - I usually have small hairs needing to be plucked each morning. Since using this equipment my morning regimen has change. No plucking. I’m sure it will be many days and they will appear again and I will attack them again. I’m not going to give up.

title: Wouldn't recommend.
text: I've used Manic Panic Amplified for years but decided to try this because Manic Panic hasn't been lasting as long as it used to. I had high hopes for this but was very disappointed. I left the dye in for at least an hour before rinsing with cool water, but the color wasn't nearly as vibrant as I expected it to be. Additionally, my scalp and face got very itchy while I had the dye in my hair. Being the fast learner that I am, I decided to try the dye again the next day. The itching on my face and scalp started much faster this time and my neck also got very itchy. Still, I assumed it was a coincidence because who's actually allergic to hair dye (despite the warnings to do a strand test)? So, a few weeks later I tried it again, and the itching started even faster that time. I decided I wasn't going to use this on my hair again but had the bright idea to squeeze some onto the back of my hand when I was applying it to my niece's hair. Within about 30 minutes my hand began to itch and burn. I don't have sensitive skin and have never had an issue with dyes before so I'm not sure what's in Sparks that isn't in Manic Panic.

title: Most comfortable electric shaver I've used
text:
As someone who's never grown a beard or mustache (I can go maybe a day or two but after that it drives me crazy), this has to be the most comfortable electric shaver I've ever used. The three pivoting heads move over facial contours very easily, and I appreciate that it's quieter than others I've used. In spite of that, it took a week or so to feel like it was doing a good job - perhaps because my face needed time to adjust (as claimed in the manual) or because it takes me a while to figure out how to use it most effectively - but I'm happy with the job it does. The neck has been the hardest area, and I've still got a few rough spots on the jawline, but it's much less than at first. The only real "negative" is the very small trimmer, which is placed rather awkwardly and doesn't seem to work very well.

Another nice plus is the LED display showing exactly how much time is left before needing to be recharged, and each charge seems to last much longer than others I've used (betweeen 2 to 3 weeks, and it only took an hour to fully charge). You can't use it plugged in, but it will allow you to plug it in for a "quick charge." The display also shows when to clean it (about every other use) and the heads flip up for easy cleaning under running water (much easier than using the tiny brush). It looks great, too - very sleek and cool and stylish - with a nice little charging stand, which looks good enough to leave out on the counter (minus the cord). It also comes with a "charging pod" which will be good for travelling.

Overall, I'm very pleased with it, although $240 is pretty steep. It's still not "as close as a blade" but I've given up believing that any electric shaver truly can be, but it's a whole lot more convenient that using shaving cream. (Others have complained about the "excessive" and difficult packaging, and I must agree completely on that point - use a knife carefully or scissors).

title: Quality quick-application filler for deep facial scars
text: I have tried using soooo many different products to fill in/obscure a deeply indented scar in my cheek. While Dermaflage is a good product, its application process is way too labor intensive for this low-maintenance gal. And most creams don't work well when filling in such a deep scar, as they don't set up solidly. Finally--success with this Ben Nye product!! First, I apply some Aveeno Baby lotion around the perimeter of the scar area. Next, I put some lotion on a little flat-head "tool" (not included) that I use to scoop out a small amount of wax and fill in the indentation. Finally, I put a small amount of lotion on my forefinger and smooth out the edges (if any) of wax extending past the indented area.  Luckily the shade of wax I chose matches my complexion almost perfectly, so I don't even have to wear makeup over it. Totally awesome.

I probably could have lived with that but the flickering lights are just too annoying. It also seems like the lights are noticeably dimmer after just 3 weeks of use. I'm not running it on batteries ... this is with the unit plugged into my wall outlet. And pay attention to the dimensions - at 16" tall, this is one big mirror! title: Just OK
text:
I have used Norelco shavers for many years and prefer them to all other models I have tried. This review comes from much experience with the brand.

This Arcitec design looks fancy and works well, but gets three average stars because it really works no better than the previous non-space-age, less expensive designs. The 1050, 1060, and 1090 all operate the same, they just have a different display: none is better than the other, and don't waste money on the Cleaner accessory. It is fairly comfortable to hold (I prefer the feel of the original model because this is one is smaller), and looks fancy sitting on the counter, but it does not do a better job. It requires cleaning after each use as there is no space for the hairs. I have always cleaned my shavers after each use anyway. Unless you just want the apperance, it is not worth the extra money.

Technically: The triple-blade per round head (each cutter has three spinning blade areas) does not do any better than a double cutting blade. Kind of like a Saturday Night Live skit in the 70's, Jim Belushi said, "Buy a triple bladed razor. For those that will believe anything". Lift-and-cut is a ridiculous concept and a marketing ploy, but Norelco's do a nice job. Norelco went back to more diagonal openings with this model, which is good. Many other newer models have lots of little pin-holes which are worthless, as the hairs have no time to enter a small hole with shaving motion. You would have to set it directly on a hair to cut it. The less pin-holes the better.

Feel: The head design feels better on the neck, and has not caused razor-burn if I do not stretch the skin tight like I need to with the original model. It takes the same time to shave as my older unit, and I get no better results, no faster. It takes the same time to get a smooth shave, and the same persistance in some areas.

Function: It is a little bit easier to shave under the nose, above my mustache, but not a major difference. It is much harder to trim my sideburns with this unit as the heads almost cut my hair on my head above the ears the way it is shaped. Very poorly designed trimmer, so I use my older shaver for that.

Battery: Early, new battery life is good, as with most new battery devices. But, it states to not over-charge. Meaning, you need to be around when recharging to unplug it after it is charged; not good for those that shave and must go to work. The other units would shut off after charging.

Cleaner/Charger: Again, one needs to be around when it is finished to not over-charge. The cleaner is not necessary and a waste of time, money, and chemicals. I use a razor brush from an older unit, brush it out quickly over the wastebasket (one can't blow it out without getting hair dust all over the bathroom, and it can't be shaken into the wastebasket as easily as the older models), and I spray it quickly with Remington Silicone Lubricant (I can only find it at Wal-Mart, which is the only thing I ever go there for, by the way).

Overall: Buy it for looks and keep your old one in the drawer for sideburns. Nicer on the neck.

I would return it, BUT, it was a gift, AND I like the look of it. I know, I know!!

Update 12-29-07
I purchased a Braun 9595 through J&R on Amazon, and it has a 60 day trial return guarantee. This is the first micro-screen razor I have used and it is better in all respects to the Norelco Arcitec except for the fancy styling. It is fast and comfortable, and my face feels razor smooth. I was able to return the Arcitec and am really happy with this Braun 9595 model.

I'm on my third bottle of this beard oil and I'm very happy with it. I like to grow full bore mountain man beards, especially during ski season, and this stuff keeps my beard feeling soft, controls stray hairs, and even gives it a richer, fuller color than when it's dry. No joke - some random guy in the car next to me at a stop light yesterday motioned to where his beard would be, pointed at me, and gave me a thumbs up. I think Flapwings deserves some of the credit for that.

Also, why is it that 90% of my beard compliments are from other guys? Can't blame Flapwings for that but it’s a thing. I've had at least three people yell "nice beard!" or some variation of that while driving past. Am I being cat called? Is this sexual harassment? I don't know the answers but if you want a beard that compels strangers to yell compliments at you then give this stuff a try.

title: I Just Had The Best Dental Checkup Of My Life!
text: I've been using my Oral B Vitality toothbrush for just over 6 months. I just had my 1st dental exam after using it, & it was the best ever! My dentist said my gums have never looked better, I didn't have to have ANY cleaning at all!! They just did my exam & polished my teeth. When I asked about the cleaning, the hygienist said I didn't need any cleaning because I did not have anything to be cleaned off! WOW! I love how the heads on the brush have bristles that stick out further on the outer row to get your gumline & between your teeth cleaned. It also has a 2 minute timer which doesn't reset itself if you stop for a few seconds to spit. I love this toothbrush & I hope Oral B never stops carrying it. I started using it after my previous brand's heads became harder & harder to find, & the price kept going up to almost $8/head! I called Oral B customer service & was referred to this brush. The rep said that this brush head only goes around & doesn't pulsate, so it's easier on your teeth. I had used a more expensive Oral B model for a few years, but it was just too hard on my teeth, & they became super sensitive. That's not the case with the brush. I love it! The Amazon price can't be beat either.

title: Typical foot cream ingredients, but with Colloidal Silver added
text: According to the product label, the purpose of the Silver Miracles Colloidal Silver Foot Cream is to hydrate, lubricate, moisturize, and soothe dry skin, AND kill odor-causing bacteria. From what I can tell, it seems to do this, but not in a dramatic fashion. The ingredients include Almond Oil, Cocoa Seed Butter, Shea Butter, Coconut Oil, Urea, Peppermint Oil, Menthol, and Colloidal Silver. As others have pointed out, the cream spreads easy enough but doesn't seem to absorb that well into the skin. Also, the dinky-sized jar only contains 2-ounces. So, you'll probably go through the bottle fairly quickly if you follow the instructions and apply liberally. It's a decent foot cream overall but seems a little pricey for the small amount you get.

text: I am a lifelong user of Norelco electric razors (20+ years). I have 3 of them all working. I've had this one for close to 3 years and almost never use it. It shaves poorly, because it cannot be cleaned properly. On the older models you can remove and clean the individual blades. After a week of use, on the older models you can see they need thorough cleaning as residue accumulates at the cutting  edge. This can only be done with a brush, AFTER you remove the blade. The Arcitec doesn't allow you to do this. No amount of rinsing in the water will clean the Arcitec heads. Those who say "cleans easily" don't realize that rinsing the cutting head only cleans it superficially. Also as others noted the razor will not hold the whiskers when shaving and will make a mess. The chambers for clippings storage are ridiculously small and you need to empty them after each use. No matter how many passes you take, it will miss some hairs.

title: Great for Spa Nights at Home!
text:
I love these! They puff up in liquid like little marshmallows, and are easy to peel apart to form the full mask. It works for my face size/shape just fine (a smidge small for men maybe). They appear to be high quality, and I love that they're individually wrapped! I throw these in my travel bags to use with my serums and lotions, and give them away for people interested. I've used these with blended aloe vera, gels, serums, hyaluronic acid, high moisture "lotions" and so much more.

I usually remove the packaging, drop the "tablet" into the well-mixed up liquid/gel concoction I chose that day, and wait for it to puff up. Then I peel it apart to form the full mask, plop it back in the liquid/gel to absorb some more, and throw it in the fridge (because I like the masks cold). I use these after showering and cleaning my face, and watch tv or read with it on. Once I feel the edges start to feel slightly dry, I peel it off and massage the rest of it into my skin, and top off with another moisturizer before bed.

I liked these so much, I bought a package for my mom too! Do note, the ones I received were actually in a blue bag, with blue writing on the individual packaging.

title: One time use.  NOT reusable.
text:
Problem #1- I bought these because they are stated to be reusable. I assure you that they are NOT. The attached microbead is literally glued onto the extension, so you cant even use a new microbead. Whenever you install these (mine were installed by a professional), the attached beads crush, so they CANNOT be moved up (nor replaced with a new bead as the other is glued on).

Problem #2- Due to the way these are made (Your hair is pulled through a smooth tube and the microbead clamps over it), they slide right out of your hair with the slightest bit of pressure. On the first night I had 56 extensions loose and sliding out. I then had to go pay to have another box installed. FIFTY SIX.

Problem #3- the ends of the extension (closest to your scalp) are frayed edges. Instead of being keratin bonded and smooth, they are like little brooms of sharp hairs that constantly itch your scalp. I mean, insanely uncomfortable.

I attempted to contact the seller. She stated she would send me a new pack of beads to try. She never sent them. It wouldn't have mattered anyway because she has clearly never seen the product or she would realize that you can't use a new microbead with these as here is literally no way to get the old, broken bead off.

These were purchased for approximately $325. I spent another $300 to have them installed. $625 to have them in for less than a week. If you want to spend over $600 for a week of slipping extensions, then these are for you.

title: So Much More than Just an Air Freshener
text: I never in my life would have thought I'd consider an air freshener as a giftable item, except perhaps as a gag gift to one of my brothers. This Florona Hibiscus Air Freshener, however, is in a class all by itself. Even the box it comes in is beautiful, with soft sweeping white and beige brush strokes in the background and beautiful flowers embossed in gold. The pattern wraps all the way around the box, with each side being different. The inside of the box is a vivid lime green. The spray bottle for the air freshener looks more like a perfume bottle, light and dark pink with more of the gold embossing, this time with leaves instead of flowers. The key ingredients of this air freshener are water, hibiscus oil, and hydrogenated castor oil. The bottle contains 3.38 fl oz (100 ml). The directions say to wash linens and clothes before spraying this linen mist on them. It says this spray can be used on sheets, furniture fabric, drapes, pillows, and directly in the room as an air freshener. It also says to shake the bottle well before using it. When sprayed, the hibiscus scent is very subtle and faint, but definitely there. My wife had me spray some on the blouse she was wearing so that she could enjoy the hibiscus scent all day long, and she plans to spray her pillow before she goes to sleep. For a soft, gentle hibiscus aroma that stays pleasantly in the background without overwhelming my senses, this Florona Hibiscus Air Freshener works well.

title: NO DESIGN FLAW - USER ERROR - Well Designed, Its design is INTENTIONAL
text: I would give 6 stars if possible. For only $25.00 this is an extremely well engineered item. I also have an ORIGINAL Sonicare Advanced and it is nearly 13 years old now.  I learned a long time ago how damaging water can be.  My brother in law and my sister got Sonicare brushes for themselves and me for Christmas over a decade ago. Theirs went out quick, due to water and calcium buildup. All my Sonicare products I take extreme care of.  When using, I try and never let water drip down the brush head.  After use, I remove the brush head, and store it and the handle upside down.  This keeps water from going down into the device or the threads and causing problems.  If not stored upside down, not only can water get into the item, but, calcium and contaminants can leak down and get in the brush head threads.  All can be resolved by simple care and upside down storage. Of note, my original Series A over a decade old, looks like NEW from pampered care, excluding the discoloration of age. A little care goes a LONG way. A little lack of care/abuse and anything will deteriorate. With the top of the line Sonicare Diamond Clean being over $200.00 versus the HX3351 at only $25, you can't expect bullet proof.  For $300 to $400 they could make this out of aircraft aluminum like LED Flashlights and make it 100% waterproof but you wouldn't buy it, the cost would be prohibitive.  A little care goes a LONG way, 13 years for me and counting.

The buttons are also in the perfect position where you can even put the lower part of the ear loop on your ear if you wanted. The buttons are not too high like some other headbands.

THESE ARE PERFECT!!!! If I could scream it from the top of a building I totally would. I am in love with these headbands and they are far superior to any others that I have tried - seriously and truly. TOTALLY RECOMMEND!!! title: Good soap, good value
text:
First, I have to echo the other reviewer's sentiment about the picture being misleading. The text does state that you're buying a single stick, but the picture makes it look like you're buying a box of 'em.

I was initially disappointed with my [[ASIN:B002A5OLHQ Arko Shaving Soap Stick]]. I prefer using a brush and mug, so I unwrapped the stick, cut it into shaving soap coins (which I believe are legal tender in Venezuela and Costa Rica, and certain counties in Arkansas), put them into my mug and stuck it in the microwave for 30 seconds to melt them. What a mistake! That's what I do with [[ASIN:B001A3DNY6 Van Der Hagen Deluxe Shave Soap, 2.5-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 12)]], and it works great--the soap liquifies, adheres to the bottom and sides of the mug, and once it resolidifies, I'm good to go. This stuff softened, yes, but it didn't really liquify. Instead, it kind of foamed up and over the top of the mug. I tried to use my knife to moosh it back into the mug, but it resulted in a very rough, craggy top surface. Figured I'd just use the brush on it anyway--eventually I would wear down that rough surface and leave behind a smooth top that would work perfectly.

I'm glad I did! This soap lathered up like nobody's business when I put the warm, moist brush to it. It made a little thinner lather than I usually get from VDH, but despite it's thinness, it wasn't runny at all--it stayed right where I brushed it, and didn't dry out before my razor got to it. My habit is to do three shaves in different directions, and the Arko lather held out for all three.

I used an older blade, an [[ASIN:B001QY8QXM 100 Astra Superior Premium Platinum Double Edge Safety Razor Blades]], probably about two shaves past when I would usually have replace it, but I was being lazy. I kind of expected to nick myself a couple times. But with the Arko shaving soap, that did not happen. My blade glided across my face as smoothly as I could ask. No nicks at all.

As many have mentioned, the scent is kind of strong, and not entirely pleasant. Some have compared it to citronella, which I can understand. I though it was kind of a cross between the original Old Spice scent from when my dad used their cologne, and some industrial chemical smell. VDH is more or less unscented, so I usually mix a few drops of my favorite cologne ([[ASIN:B00AATHC52 ASPEN by Coty]]) into the liquid when it comes out of the microwave, but I didn't think that would be a good idea with Arko. I didn't think the cologne scent would mix well with the cloying scent the soap came with. But while the scent isn't my cup of tea, after you rinse your face well, it's not very strong at all, especially if you wash your face afterward and use a scented after-shave balm.

The bottom line is, for a single stick, the value isn't great. It's a lot more affordable, and just as usable a product, as some of the much more expensive soaps out there, but at the single stick price, plus shipping, you could buy several pucks of Van Der Hagen locally. When you buy more sticks at a time, the per-stick price comes down, and if you can manage to get free shipping in the deal, it starts looking really attractive. The lather is very nice, the scent, not so much, but all in all, you can't go too far wrong with it. I'm dinging it one star only because of the scent. While I would rather it liquify in the microwave like VDH, that's not how the product was designed to be used, so it wouldn't be fair to dock it for that.

title: Excellent quality. Very attractive assortment of confetti and glitter polishes. Small bottles.
text:
This is an excellent assortment of attractive, quality gel polish, with base coat and top coat (no wipe and matte versions). The presentation elevates the worth as a gifting option. One of the best features of this set is the bottles of gel polish provide actual representations of the contents. On the top of the cap is a sticker with proper identification of the color (including glitter, confetti varieties). Also on the front label is a small circle, under the brand name, that has an accurate portrayal of the contents. Finally, a company gets it. So, this boosts the rating a bit.
There are 16 varieties of gel polish, with confetti and glitter. The consistency is perfect, allowing application of a thin coat. An ability to apply a thin coat is essential, mainly due to the size of these bottles. They are 5ml (0.17fl oz), which is one of the smallest bottles of gel polish that i've encountered. This is actually a disappointing size/quantity of gel polish. Based on the size of the bottles of gel polish, I consider this to actually be a sampler set, as opposed to a set of 'normal' sized bottles of attractive confetti, glitter gel polishes. So, i guess one can try out the various polishes and then purchase larger bottles of desired varieties.
Overall, I feel all of the polishes in the assortment are visually attractive. The polishes are excellent quality, and produce excellent results. The significant other tested the gel on some fake nails, and i commented that it looks beautiful as is...no need to add any embellishments/decorations. She agreed., but said she'd combinde solid color gel polish with these, and add some rhinestones or other decorations, maybe create a marbled effect. I could see her creative juices flowing.
The product does match the descriptions and pictures in the product details. The presentation is effective and top notch. We like the quality and consistency of the gel polishes and base/top coats. If the bottles were at least the size of the base/top coat bottles, this set would be perfect and worth 5 stars. As currently configured, based on observations and impressions, this quality 'sampler' set earns a 4.5 - 4.75 star rating, with a high level recommendation.
