title: Really Like the Clean Feeling!
text:
I bought this product about a month ago and have really liked it so far. I really feel that my mouth feels cleaner after I brush with this toothbrush. It has also helped me brush longer and more thuroughly. It gives you a warning every 30 seconds, telling you when to switch sides of your mouth. While I don't always use this, it has helped me to brush for a full two minutes.
Each charge last for a long time as well. I actually can't remember the last time I charged my toothbrush. I would say once every couple weeks, and I brush multiple times a day.
The only con I have noticed is that the tooth brush head is alittle small, but the vibration and cleaning power of the toothbrush makes this less noticeable.
I would recommend this toothbrush to anyone looking for a better toothbrush that wants a cleaner mouth and whiter teeth.

title: Norelco finally got it right!
text:
I have used electric razors for over 35 years all have good points and all have weaknesses. Norelco razors have two problems.

Hard to clean. As the cutter and blades fill up the motor is not able to maintain the speed and nicks result. You must take the blade and cutter assembly apart to clean properly and if you cut longer hairs by accident(side burn area etc.) it will wrap around the cutting blade and really slow the motor down. The motor slowing is the worst on the new "3 blade" called the Speed XL, they just seem to be underpowered.(just as a note the older Spectra blade replacements fit these razors and seem to work better). Think of this problem like a paper shredder that has not beeen emptied and hasn't room for more papers in the bin.

Hidden Build up. I have changed batteries in several of my Norelco shavers and the build up of gunk where only disassembly could find and cure it, is remarkable(same with most brands). I suspect this is gunk is high in bacteria and the cause of most skin infections from nicks. The stuff you never knew was there is nasty!

Now for the cure: Norelco 8060x! This razor blade assembly has open backs for the stubble to fall through as you shave. I use mine in the shower so no mess. The blade assembly is completely seperate from the motor unit= no place for gunk to build up! The unit is entirely water proof. The blades disassemble easily so you can get at those occasional "longer hairs" that wrap around the cutters. The cutters have more blades on each, so the shave is faster and the motor seems stronger than previous models, hence less slowing. The Nivea solution is great, but you can use regular shave cream or gel. I like the Nivea Gel for Sensitive Skin, it seems to be the right thickness for the razor and works alomost as well as the cream you load in the razor.

The only negative for some people might be the size or shape of the handle, it is smaleer and lighter than most shavers. Some people with larger hands may find is inconvient and others may find it easy to drop.

Overall this is the best all around razor I have owned, there is a brief time required for your face to get used to any new razor... This ones worth it!!

title: Sets make-up on my extremely sensitive skin without irritation
text:
I purchased this product because I needed something to help me set my foundation without the use of powder. I have sensitive dry skin, large pores, and some mild rosecea (sp.?). I use a sunscreen to protect me from getting sunburned and a liquid foundation to cover up the ruddiness. However, when I tried to use powder to set my make-up, it would settle into my pores and make me look ashy and "drag-queeny."

The product does what it is supposed to do. Just make sure you spray it 12" to 16" away from your face. Any closer and you wind up with sticky spots. Also use only 1 to 3 sprays to set your make-up. Anything more than that and again, you'll wind up with a sticky feeling. I mist it on my face after I put on my eyeliner and mascara. It helps prevent me from getting raccoon eyes.

Overall, I like this product and would recommend it if you are looking for an alternative to powder setting your make-up.

title: Sizzling Bath Bombs are the best!
text:
The sensation of the salts reacting to the water, the color it makes your bathwater into, the lovely scents and the Epsom salts making your skin feel silky smooth. We love everything about that!

With that in mind, we have tried multiple types of bath bombs and here is our experience:

Many times, you want "full strength" bath bombs in your tub, because the salts are what makes your hands and skin super soft and amazing, and there could be never be "too much" stuff in the water for you.

These particular bath bombs are fizzy.

These turn the bath a highly saturated color that the ball is.
Overall, we like this set a whole lot for adults, not kids.

title: I love the BeautyWill Professional Makeup Brush set
text: I love the BeautyWill Professional Makeup Brush set, It arrived a week ahead of scheduled. It comes in a resealable package which is a plus. It did have a kind of strong smell when you first open the package but just let it air out for about an hour and it will be gone. It comes with 32 brushes total and that goes from bronzing, foundation, and eye-shadow brushes. It also includes a brush for your lips and eyebrows. It is wonderful and complete set. I purchased the golden natural kit and it is lovely. Good quality bristles so far there has been no shedding. I do prefer the more fluffy and natural brushes but am very happy with the synthetic foundation brush great for liquids. The pouch they come in is great you can roll it up and tie the strings and be on the go in seconds. When you are on the go and in a hurry to get to your next job every second counts. The way they are sitting inside the case is great very organized, you can find what you are looking for very quickly which is important. I really can't say enough good things about this set. So if you are looking for a set to use with both liquid and dry foundation or if you need to apply glitter or face paint I think this set would be a good match for that. Hope that my review has helped someone make there decision and if you have an questions ask away lol.

text:
I had never had "go-to" deodorant as they are mostly very cheaply made and hardly ever give a feeling of satisfaction. Those that smell nice, leave residue. Those that are clothes-friendly, smell like a sour milk with lysol in it. A very few with great smell are so hard, they will scratch off your skin when applying.

This deodorant is actually very-very nice. It has non-abusive fresh smell with notes of tea. No sour milk/lysol with powder notes. It does not require pumping hard. One pump delivers nice diameter of spray with enough force. It feels freah, clean, happy. Although it has smell, it will not overpower your perfume.

Unfortunately, it is hard to get in USA. Even though amazon sells it, it takes months to arrive and a few times it did not arrive.

title: Easy to apply.  No irritation.  Great price.
text: So far, so good.  I have used many different lash serums, including Rodan & Fields.  I've only used this product for three days and so far there is no irritation.  If you've never used a lash serum before, it is very easy to put on.  It's almost like applying clear eye liner.  Most lash serums suggest putting on before bed at night.  I can never remember when I go to bed, so I put mine on in the morning when I'm applying my makeup.  For me, this works fine.  I just let it dry for a minute and put my eye liner over it.  I have done that routine with this product and it has done fine.  As with the other ones I've tried, I will also be using it in my eye brows.  In three days I have not noticed a huge difference in the length (I have long lashes anyway), but I have also not noticed any irritation or loss of lashes like I've seen in some.  This is also a great price for eye lash serum.

title: Perfect for key chain, purse strap, belt loop
text:
These little bottles are exactly what I wanted for hand sanitizer on the go. The silicone bottle is durable and thick, but so easy to squeeze. It isn't going to break and crack like a hard plastic bottle. The cap fits securely and the snap-top stays in place. The carabiner is small, but it's strong enough to keep the bottle where it needs to be as long as there isn't serious weight constantly pulling on it. So far, so good.

The thing I worry about most with a little bottle like this is what happens if you put a lot of pressure on it (sit on it, lean against the wall when it's in a pocket or on a belt loop, etc). I was concerned that the flip top would pop open and I'd end up with hand sanitizer all over the place, but I tested these little guys full of liquid before filling with sanitizer. The pliability of the silicone doesn't allow a burst of pressure to push off the flip top-- not even close. Plus, the top screws tightly into place. It's not going anywhere.

I've been using one of these hanging from my purse strap for the last few days and it is super easy to flip the cap, squeeze out product, and flip the cap closed with one hand. Wish there were other color options, but the functionality and design of these override any concerns about color. I'm so glad I ordered these! They are totally worth the price.

title: Awesome product, great results!
text: I am in love with skin care, more than ever in my life now that I in my 50s. I truly wish I started in my earlier years. And the newest & most exciting things in skin care are Korean skin care products. I had heard they were the best so I was excited to see this 2-step product & even more excited to try it. I will admit, I am a bit obsessed with face masks at the moment. I use a mask every day, especially during this pandemic while I am stuck at home & not able to go to a cosmetologist for a facial. I have aged & have dry skin with lots of fine wrinkles appearing around the corners of my eyes and a deep nasolabial fold forming. I am saddened every time Iook at my reflection in the mirror. I absolutely love this 2-step product. I opened one end of the product & applied the small masks to my eyes & around my mouth (4 to a pack) and then applied the whole face mask (step 2) over it all. I left them all on according to the directions and my face felt amazing afterwards. I was so pleased with the way my face felt that I was already looking forward to the next time I used this product. Btw, my face is very sensitive to skin care products & this particular product did not cause me any redness, irritation,  peeling or burning.  I would not hesitate to purchase this product in the future for a regular skin care regiment.

title: Good stuff
text: Love it. It lathers really well even in hard water, cushions my slant razor, rinses easily without leaving a greasy film, and my wife loves the scent. What's not to like? I did not expect this from a puck of hard soap but the triple milling works. Fortunately, it fit into my lathering bowl almost perfectly. All I had to do was use my pocket knife to chamfer the edge of the puck so it would fit st the bottom where the bowl curves inward. Left the shavings in the bottom. Do not melt or microwave as that will destroy the benefits of milling and leave you with something that will not lather. By the way, my technique for lather is to simply moisten the top of the puck with my brush before I shower. Once I get out, I prepare the brush with hot water in the usual way and go to work. The lather jumps up with just a few swirls. I use a Satin Tip synthetic brush that is not stiff.

title: Loved this product. (Yes, it's just a stippling brush, but better than DIY.)
text:
I can't rave enough about this product. I've had it for a couple months now, and I've used it with mostly eyeshadow, but all sorts of colors. It's removed them all pretty much instantly! I will say that the only problem with this is that it will probably make people forget that even though it's taking off the pigment, it's not removing bacteria and such, so you should probably still wash your brushes once or twice a month.

And yes. I know that it's just a stippling brush in a little can. So if you want to DIY it, I'm sure you could. I find this a lot more convenient than trying to spend an afternoon coming up with a copy of this. Part of its use is the can: the stippling part doesn't move around (well, it rotates a little) because it's contained in the can. So I just have it sitting in a corner of my makeup box, and I can just brush my brushes towards the inside corner, and it doesn't move. Alternatively, you could put some hot glue or silicone on the top face of the lid, so when you put the lid on the bottom (like the picture) and had it on your countertop, it wouldn't slide, either.

Anyways, love this product! Also works with bigger brushes. I'm wishing I'd bought the duo, though, because it's supposed to work with creme make up.

title: Love the Coconut Baby & will try others...
text: ***  Just received the Limited Edition "Sugar Cookie" in the mail today.  My 21-month-old daughter is already asking for a bath while yelling "yummy" -- yes, it smells great! *****  I first tried Sweet Baby Organics Shampoo & Wash in Coconut Baby for my daughter last fall when she was 10 months old.  I liked the scent right away, but I was also impressed with the fact that it rinsed so clean.  I have tried other natural and organic baby washes with a coconut base, and they seemed to leave a "film" on my baby -- I liked that this formula was rich, and thick, without being sticky.  It's true that the foaming is minimal when comparing Sweet Baby Organics to washes with chemical lathering agents (such as SLS) but I found the lather from the coconut base to be "just right" without being bubbly.  We live in Kenya, so when I buy on Amazon I have to wait for my family to send me a box in the mail, or for someone to come over to visit (last fall I brought a supply of it back in my luggage).  My next package arrives in April, and this time we are trying the Vanilla Baby scent. The shampoo is NOT tear-free, but again, that is because of the lack of numbing chemicals in it's formulation -- I simply hold one hand over my daughter's forehead, and rinse her hair with a cup of clean water in my other hand.  One other fact to note is that a little goes a long way.  At first I was concerned that there were only 8oz bottles available, but even at 15 months old, a dime-sized amount cleans my daughter's entire head and body (though I have to admit that being a blondie, she doesn't have a whole lot of hair yet).

title: Been using for years
text:
I bought my first box years ago (when I seem to remember it being the old packaging being labeled as brow safe?). I just finished up another box (3rd? 4th?) and noticed that it’s not meant to be used on the brows. It’s not difficult to keep far away from your eyes, just exercise caution.

I used to apply it for the recommended couple of minutes and got decent results, but now that I’m used to the performance I’ve left it on for as long as 40 minutes and have had the same color payoff but much longer lasting color. I have clear blonde brows despite being a natural dark brunette, so anything I can do to prevent fading helps.

Recommendations:
- Block/outline your brows with a white eyeliner before applying so you can just fill in the shape without having to be too precise with the actual dye- this also keeps skin staining within the area of the brow and less noticeable
- Dye at night or when you can stay in for a couple of days if you’re worried about skin staining
- Dont exfoliate the day of dyeing and the skin stain will come up easier
- use a cotton bud lightly dipped in skin salve/Vaseline to scrub up skin stain rather than washing it away to avoid early fading on brow hairs- even just applying a bit to brows and leaving it on for a while before wiping it away can really get it off much easier AND avoid irritating the skin
- wipe away areas you want to be lighter half way through your dyeing process (Ex: if you want the inner part of the brow to be lighter than the tail, wipe away some dye on the inner part and leave more to develop on the tail)
- You can reapply dye if you feel it didn’t tint enough in one session. Just leave any you didn’t use up on the counter and only discard extra after you’re satisfied with the color payoff (don’t save dye for another day, just go ahead and reapply right after taking off the first application if you feel you want more color)

title: easy for sensitive teeth
text:
GLO Science Teeth Whitening Pen 35 Aplications, Safe and Efective, No Sensitivity, Easy to Use Dentist Invented Whitening Treatment, Travel-Friendly

Are my teeth whiter? Yes. It's not so white that anyone will see me and be like 'wow your teeth are so white!' but it is whiter and I notice it, which I prefer. I have very sensitive teeth and almost no enamel. The pen is a click pen, and you have click quite a lot to get it started. I thought mine was faulty at first, and the clicker even got stuck half way down but I smacked it and it popped back and I kept clicking. The silicone tip has little bristles and it does a good job of coming off on your teeth - I was worried the gel wouldn't grip onto my teeth and would slide, but it didn't. It does have a cinnamon taste, and it does dry in 30 seconds, it was easy enough to smile for 30 seconds and it was on there. Truly I forgot about it after that (don't eat or drink for 30 min if you can). Are my teeth whiter? I couldn't tell after 1 use, but after two, I also couldn't tell... BUT I took a before and after photo (which I'll save you from because my lips look awful right now) and my teeth are definitely visibly whiter! I'm happy with this, it's easy to put on, doesn't make my teeth sensitive, works for whitening, dries quickly, and you don't even have to spit it out.

title: The best lubricating, least drying shave soap I've found
text: This is the reasonably-priced refill for the ceramic jar (which is expensive).  It fits the jar with a little room to spare, in case you have some remaining soap clinging to the perimeter of the jar.  A couple of uses will fill the remaining void..  I've found that on the first use, filling the jar to the brim with warm water a few minutes before shaving will condition the soap so it lathers much more easily.  Then, pour off the water before using.  This only needs to be done ONCE with a new puck of soap - if you cover the jar with the lid between uses.  It lathers easily, with very rich and lubricating, very lightly scented, foam.  Yet, it rinses from a DE razor quite easily.  I favor the scent of the Taylor of Bond Street Sandalwood Soap, but this product is more lubricating.  This is an excellent product.

title: Highly Recommended
text:
I replaced a different manufacturer's very old electric razor with the Arcitec 1050, and have been using it for about three weeks. My primary shaving method has been the razor blade, supplemented by the electric for a fast shave now and then. I am so satisfied with the new Norelco that it has become my primary shaver. It delivers results virtually as close as a blade, and sometimes I think it is just as good. I don't feel that I need to use a blade to get a close shave any more, and I'm really amazed by the results. I didn't think that an electric could ever come this close to a blade. I guess that the technology has improved over the last twenty years, like everything else!

The shaver is not perfect, but the disadvantages are minor in my opinion. It does produce hair shavings, so I just shave over the sink. I don't mind cleaning up a little. The trimmer is quite small, but it does work well enough to clean the sideburns with an up and down motion. I have found that the shave is quite comfortable if you don't overdo it - just shave until you are clean, and use an after-shave cream to avoid minor irritation. I would prefer being able to leave the plug in the shaver, rather than remove it right after the charge is complete. It's a small inconvenience for such a great shave. And it is more economical than blades in the long run.

title: Poor shave
text: I had owned a Norelco rotary shaver years ago (1970s) and it worked well; eventually the battery would not take a charge (rechargeables back then were always having this problem). I replaced it with a regular (non-rotary) rechargeable electric razor. When that one finally died, I tried a Norelco rotary again--and it would not give me a good shave and I returned it. I went through a few more non-rotary electric razors since then, usually Remington models and one Braun (the latter was really nice, but expensive and also expensive to maintain as it was one of the self-cleaning models and the cleaning solution was expensive and never lasted quite as long as they claimed). My current razor, a Remington non-rotary has seen better days so I decided to get a new one and tried this Norelco rotary. Big mistake (I should have learned from my last Norelco rotary shaver experience!). I have a tough beard but VERY sensitive skin. I could not get a close shave with this shaver. I had to keep going over the same area multiple times and it still did not shave as well as the old Remington model (which has not had the blades replaced in over a year). Now I'm stuck with this piece of junk, since Amazon doesn't take back opened merchandise.

title: A little flimsy for multiple 8 inch Jo Jo bows.
text:
My 7yr old, and I were so excited to get these to hold her many, many large Jo Jo Siwa bows. I was expecting the ribbon to be a little thicker, and longer. It holds 4 large 8 inch bows comfortably. Possibly 5, however because the ribbon is really thin, it doesn't hang straight when holding, organizing, or using. The ribbon twists around, and doesn't hang straight at all. There just isn't enough space for what we need either. I think these would be great for smaller bows, tho. They look just as pictured. I'm just now learning about the different types of ribbon out there to hold the larger bows more stable. I have not found or purchased a new set, yet. I think if these were longer, it might not have been a complete miss for what I was looking for. The set with the crowns and the ribbon rings as the hooks, do fit over my daughter's dresser draw knobs, which I was hoping, and looking for when ordering. I'm still giving them a 3 star review, because they would be nice for other's needs as I've mentioned. A suggestion would be to make much longer ones. Another foot or 2, and possibly use a heavier ribbon material.

I'm not upset with the product itself, as much as I am upset that I didn't realize that these are not ideal for a lot of large bows. They are pretty!

title: Love these
text:
I absolutely love these hand sanitizers. The problem I have with so many hand sanitizers is that they are either a pump or a squirt bottle and spill so easily! I use hand sanitizer constantly so I have to carry them with my. I have had so many spill in my purse or in my son's sports bags that I've lost count. My absolute favorite thing about these is that they are sprays! It's so easy for my kids to just spray some on and go. This stuff dries very quickly and you aren't left rubbing your hands together for a minute straight to get it to absorb. My son has had this in his baseball bag for over a week now with no spills. The containers themselves are very compact. Another thing I really like about these is that the citrus smell is not super alcoholy. Some hand sanitizers smell like vodka (seriously, because it such a strong alcohol smell) and this one actually has a very pleasant scent to it. This has got to be hands down one of my favorite hand sanitizers I've ever used.

title: Great set of gentle clippers
text:
This is an excellent set of clippers for more than just younger children. My nephew is on the spectrum and has trouble with various sensations and noise, so haircuts have always been an exercise in patience.

Unlike other hair clippers, the sound level with these is lower and less irritating to him. The ceramic blades and guides are also more gentle on skin. He's been nicked before by barbers and stylists, and that has only added to this apprehension of getting his hair cut. The blades on this set of clippers isn't as sharp as what you'd find in a barbershop (probably because they are ceramic versus steel), so you do have to make a couple more passes to cut hair and to get a close shave on the neck, but that's okay because he doesn't fear being cut by these clippers. The blade covers are also smoother without any edges from the plastic molds or those sharp little nodes from where plastic was injected into the molds or where the guides were snapped apart. It's almost as if they went through a sanding process. It's a little thing, but then again it's not, you know?

Battery life is pretty good as well. One full charge is more than enough to get through a haircut even if the person has thick hair.

title: Never going back to regular mascara again
text: I've always been afraid to try the fiber lash mascara sets for the worry that they would be way to time consuming and hard to apply compared to your everyday mascara. Boy was I wrong, I can't believe the simplicity of using the  3d fiber mascara. After using this i believe that it is actually quicker and way easier than applying layer after layer of standard mascara to get big results. Application is a super simple it's a 3 step process and the kit has clear directions for application. The mascara that comes with the kit works alone to make the lashes look good for days your not feeling like getting huge lashes. The fibers are easy to apply and the wand for applying them picks up a great amount of fibers each time it is dipped in the tube. I found that using the 3d mascara fiber set is a cleaner process than layering mascara as well because there is far less clumping which in turn means less time brushing lashes and cleaning up the fragments that fall all over your freshly done makeup. Needless to say I will probably always chose a fiber lash over a standard mascara any day. The Grace & Stella 3d fiber lashes set arrives in a very classy decorative box which shows that the manufacturer takes pride in their product as well as makes storing this kit easy . This entire kit makes having thin short eyelashes not seem to bad after all and is definitely worth repurchasing.

title: I don't really have time to write too many reviews of products I've bought so I only write reviews for products that are either
text: I bought this product probably about a month ago. I don't really have time to write too many reviews of products I've bought so I only write reviews for products that are either really great or really bad usually. I saw this product WORK! Right before my eyes. I am in my mid 30's and have always had freckly skin and the sun makes them get bigger and run together but I've now started to get age spots. They appear out of nowhere and they're big and darker than my freckles.Coincidentally, after receiving this product and using it for a few days, I woke up one morning with a big, brown AGE SPOT right under my eye. I was really upset about it as it was very noticeable. I was using Wonderpiel for a few days morning and night already when the spot appeared, so I continued using it, making sure to apply to the spot as well. I wore concealer to hide this thing and it was noticeable again by the end of each day when the concealer wore off. But one morning (9 days after the spot's arrival) I woke up and didn't see the spot. So I washed my face again, thinking I must have left the concealer on overnight, but it was GONE! I know I'm being dramatic, but I really am having trouble aging gracefully and am very self-conscious. Wonderpiel really made me happy, and is one of the few products I've used that has basically worked magic for me. Now, I do still have freckles and "discolorations" and uneven skin tone, but I've had that all my life so I don't expect this product to work as fast as it did on my brand-new age spot. I THINK I see them fading a bit but want to keep using until I'm sure of it. But, for all who are wondering whether or not to buy this product, do it. I saw it work, and will be buying another bottle.

title: Immediate Results
text: I've used a similar product prior to ordering the Melao Lavender Scented Foot Peel Mask. The best part is that this brand MELAO is HALF the cost of the brand my nail salon first turned me on to, with same desired results! I followed their instructions, soaking my feet prior to slipping them into the booties and securing with the adhesive tabs. I then covered with a pair of socks to ensure I achieved maximum contact of my feet to the activated gel in the booties (not part of their instructions). Waited the recommended hour and rinsed thoroughly. No adverse or allergic reactions or sensitivity . Peeling shown in pics began around 36 hours later. I'm still in the peeling process, but I know I'm going to love the smooth results, just in time for sandal season!  If you are a first time user, know that your feet will peel for several days so wear socks to collect the skin. And don't use too close prior to a big event where your feet will be seen. I recommend this product.

6/25/19: Update to add: In the process of cleaning the brush between uses, the clear coat/finish brush disintegrated in my hand, so it's no longer usable. Also, I have stored the items from the kit in their original bottles, well sealed and in an upright position, and the activator (step 2, a pretty important part of the process) has disappeared (evaporated, completely)!!! I'll never waste my money on the color club brand dip kits again.

title: Primal Elements Sampler Soap Grab bag...A wonderful surprise awaits.
text: I used to buy this soap by the individual bar at what I consider to be a premium price and would have to really guess if it would be an ideal scent or something I was disappointed with.  Let me tell you, they are not only beautiful and whimsical, the scents seem to go with the pics on the bar of soap.  Since they are made in long loaf type cylinders (or something like it), they cut the loafs of soaps in to bars and ship them out.  The trouble is they have so many different ones and different scents that I got tired of guessing and then discovered that they offer what they call a grab bag of different soaps that are about 3/4 of an inch thick up to about 1 inch thick.  I absolutely enjoy each sample bag I have gotten and they haven't repeated a scent yet.  They are a much better value when you buy the grab bag  and even if you don't like 1 or 2 of the scents, you can give them away as a gift on their own to someone else.  It's still got many scents to enjoy in the remaining soaps.  I was going to give this as a gift each time but I kept it for myself and bought them a different gift.  They are that good. title: Prepare to be Amazed!
text:
I moved from wet blade shaving to electrics 8 years ago now. Shaving against the grain with a blade makes a real mess of my face and shaving only with the grain doesn't give me a brilliant result. I'd figured that technolgy marches on and that electrics would be better than they were when I'd first started shaving and I was right. I was pretty happy with the remington that I bought, but after a year or so I saw a Braun activator 8585 at half price in a local store and as at the time it was close to the top of the range, I bought it. That was a revelation to me. It shaved even better and the cleaning station left it refreshed again and again. 7 years later, the battery isn't holding charge and so I looked again. I was amazed to find 'shaving forums' on the web! You guys are nuts ;) but thanks for all your craziness and for sharing the tales of your hunts for the ultimate shave!

A lot of these shaving enthusiasts were raving about the new Panasonic Arc 4 Nano wet/dry and many thought that the cleaning station version just isn't necessary as this one can be cleaned easily with liquid soap under the tap (fawcet?)(yes, I'm english). So I bought this model (8423A) and it arrived today..... Ok you want to know what it's like, so I'll tell you,,,,

- It looks great!
- It charged in about 40 mins
- It shaved off 3 days of beard in about 7 mins (I was being careful bearing in mind its a new type of shaver on my skin).
- It's shaved me SO close that I'm absolutely Amazed.
- I have no irritation (the foils just don't seem to pick up my skin at all).
- I used it dry - can't wait to try it in the shower.
- It did miss a few particularly flat hairs on my neck, but I was able to get them with the trimmer and then shave over.
- I had a go at the cleaning routine - it's not difficult.

Technology continues to march on!

Whether you go for this one, or the cleaning station version, I'm sure that you will be delighted. This shaver is FANTASTIC!

title: The best trimmer I've yet used.
text:
First off, I have to say I'm severely disappointed with some of the other reviews of this product, as I have found them to be light years from my experience with this trimmer. Thus far, I have found it to be well designed, well built and very easy to use. Also to my surprise, the vaccum works just great! Yes, there are still hairs that hit the sink, but whadda ya want, perfection? The vaccum collects easily two-thirds to three-quarters of the trimmed hair and that's a good thing.

That said, I've found the most impressive aspect of the trimmer is the extended precision trimming head. While not as wide as the standard trimming head, it extends the cutting edge far enough away from the body of the trimmer to provide the easiest and most accurate edge trimming I've ever experienced. Outlining my beard, moustache and sideburns is suddenly a far easier and more precise task. For any guy who either wants or needs cleaner lines on his facial hair, this is an excellent choice!

I'm still getting used to the length selector and how to get the desired look before having to take off the guide comb and switch trimmer heads to get a more precise cut, but I'm sure that will come with time. It operates in much the same way as my previous Norelco trimmer, but the body design of this trimmer fits much better in the hand, and the guide comb assembly provides a much more comfortable trim as I glide it over my chin. It's also a wonderful thing to have the fast 1 hour recharge time!

The only downside I've found so far is some irritation of the skin while using the extended trimmer head. It's a VERY sharp head and can nick you if you're not careful, but I put that in the still-learning-how-to-use-it category. This is truly a great trimmer, so to all my proudly facially hirsute brothers, I say don't believe the nay-sayers and pick this puppy up!

title: Disappointed - went back to my T800
text:
I bought a T800 quite a few years ago when I found the Austrian-made unit on sale at the local Target store. It lasted very well, and I was somewhat disappointed when the battery eventually would no longer accept a charge, though it continued to work while plugged in.
After carefully looking into alternatives, I went with a T970 from Amazon.com, hoping for the same quality. Major letdown! This Chinese-made unit just is not up to the same standard as my T800. The charger is a seperate seperate and bulky transformer, instead of the simple cord of the T800. The T970 is larger, more awkward to hold and to use, and more fragile in every possible way. The vacuum, which was one of the initial attractions, works, but only marginally well, and the unit is unstable when laid down on a solid surface. If the mile-long charging cord is attached, expect to have to prop the unit against a wall to prevent it rolling over.
I spent $6.00 on a top-quality replacement cell for my T800 (shipping was $4.00, for a 700mA Sanyo AA), and after careful disassembly and reassembly, successfully replaced the ailing cell without breaking anything. Unbelievably, The T800 now outlasts the T970 two-fold.
I wish I'd stuck it out with the T800 in the first place.
.
Updated June 29, 2009.
After about two years of irregular use and the resulting regular complete self-discharges, the T970 stopped functioning even while plugged in, unless first given time to recharge. Initial time to start working after plugging in has increased to the point where it no longer works even being plugged in for 24 hours.

title: This works and has no problems.

I started yanking on trim pieces that came off rather easily and set them aside. Within 2 or 3 minutes we had the dash exposed enough to remove the 4 screws that held the face plate on. Removed the old USB from the back. Installed the new one and Walla car play is working. Checked it a few times to make sure it was working and put everything back together again. As we were putting on the last piece of trim on the right, we ran the cable, hidden inside the trim to the bin. Looks professional and it’s working just fine. Maybe 15 minutes....tops.

title: Excellent value at current cost. Good quality synthetic hair. Nice ombre coloring.
text:
This wig checks off all the boxes. It has good quality synthetic hair that delivers an able semblance of authentic hair. It has nice texture and appropriate thickness. The wig exhibits good fullness/coverage, with an acceptable midline hairline. The length is as described, perhaps a slight bit longer than the advertised 21 inch length.
The ombre coloring is excellent. It goes from a blonde to a light blonde at the ends. It has a nice gradual, natural look. In fact, I might claim it's somewhere between ombre and sombre design. The wig also has highlights that adds more realism and visual appeal. Attached are photos to show real world images, with a close up of the synthetic hair to note the quality and color.
Amazingly, the current cost is just $11.50, including the 50% discount coupon. We felt it was worth the original $23, but the half price makes this an exceptional deal.
Based on the quality, visual appeal and outstanding price point, this wig easily earns a 4.75 - 5 star rating. I would heartily suggest grabbing at least one of these wigs.

title: If it ain't broke, don't fix it.
text:
I'm very disappointed in the new tube version of AHA Souffle. It has been reformulated and now has a different consistency that does not seem to absorb as well. It also leaves my skin feeling dryer than the cream version, which made my skin feel dewy for several hours after application. It also goes much faster, because you can't as easily control how much you use. Accidentally squeeze a little too much, and you have to use it, because you can't put it back in the tube.

Shame on you, Alpha Hydrox. You downgraded the best AHA product on the market.

title: Dream come true!
text:
I've always wanted my own NARS, but they're so expensive! This product performs beautifully, the bottom two blushes are matte, the highlighter has a LOT of micro-sparkles in it, the top blush (next to the highlight) has a golden sheen, and the Laguna bronzer has a satin finish. Someone wrote on here that they think they got a fake, but I don't believe I did. Heck, if I did, then the products are NARS quality so I still won't complain.

I've included some photos- the blushes should work on light to medium skin tones, but that highlighter will probably only work on light. Still, a single nars blush is just about as expensive as this whole palette. I also included a picture for sizing and one to look at the back of the palette. Hope this helps!

What I don’t like and why I didn’t give 5 stars:
1. It gets “runs” easily. You can see from my picture there’s several “runs” like would happen to a set of tights. When you’re wearing it however they’re not obvious at all.
2. This might be a no brainer because of the size but this is not really a wrist scrunchie, it’s so large wrist wear isn’t practical. You’ll need to either commit to wearing this all day or have some bag or purse to keep it in, this is slightly less practical for daily use then I personally like.
3. It’s not tight enough to be used alone, you’ll need to first use a regular hair tie and then add this for decoration, I prefer scrunchies alone because hair ties hurt after a while but this scrunchie is too slick to stay by itself and would need to be folded over several times eliminating that cute chunky oversized look that this is supposed to give.

Although I’m a little let down by the practical use potential of the product, I do like this and will definitely continue to use it for special occasions just not daily wear.

title: Contains Triclosan!
text: I used this deodorant cream years ago when I lived in Spain and was very happy with it - a bit messy to apply but definitely prevented underarm odor. And I try to avoid chemicals and aluminum! So when I saw it on Amazon I happily ordered it. When I received it and read the ingredients I noticed it contained Triclosan - an ingredient I would never knowingly use! It has not yet been taken off the market in the USA, although it has in other countries - many products are starting to eliminate it. Google it! It has been linked to hormone disregulation, the proliferation of resistant bacteria and several other serious problems. I can't believe they did not disclose on the Amazon page that the product contained this ingredient; I NEVER would have bought it!

text:
This product contains the needed ingredients to deliver results.

I have been hyper-focused on my face and realized that my neck area needs attention to look great too. This product has the ingredients needed to both moisturize and to firm up in time. After a couple weeks, I have noticed a bit of firming and my skin is definitely softer.

I have used prescription strength hydraulic acid before but it was just too harsh for the neck even with a moisturizer added. When you apply be sure to use upward strokes as you want to go against gravity and have it absorb nicely. title: Pretty stretchy and thin but OK for price
text:
These spiral ties from Gomayee are very handy. They are inexpensive. Except for the original brand of these, all of the ones I have found on Amazon have the little rough and uneven part where the the circle is completed. Thicker bands definitely have it show less though. The lower the gauge (if this is the same way earring posts are measured) the better the tie.

These are extremely thin and stretchy. I have a couple of photos with blue text that show the difference. The thick one is actually too big for my thinner hair. These are also rather loose on my thin hair. I have some smaller ones with thick plastic that are my favorites. I would think these are good for big buns and maybe for thicker hair? I can twist them around about 4 times for a pony tail. Still they don’t damage or tangle my hair and I will use them. They feel fine on my wrist too for when I take it out and don’t want to lose it.

text:
this is not the 'model' I received. I received the version with 1 large compartment on the front (as opposed to the pictured 2 compartments). I was packing for a job, and wanted to use right away so I did not contact seller to see about an exchange.

as usual, whoever makes stylist's bags does not seem to test the spaces with actual tools inside, fully loaded - as most stylist would use it. the right side has a heat resistant pocket for hot tools. it fits my flat iron & a standard length curling iron (mine must be long as it sticks out the top). difficult to use with tools still hot as the cords really have no space other than inside this pocket. there are envelope slots in the 'door' of this compartment. I have tried to fit a brush ... but it quickly becomes too bulky and difficult to zipper shut.

I use zucca cases inside the main compartment which work fine. I specifically ordered this because it was soft-sided ... but that means when fully loaded, it collapses easily and I really watch how I set it up. also, as I am using it with zucca bags (as opposed to the boxes shown) - getting into just one bag at a time is difficult since they rely on being stacked just so. my zucca bags do not protrude out of the compartment, but fit snuggly in which, again, becomes a problem when using the pockets & accompanying bags that are in this front 'door'. I usually have to lay the bag back to get it zipped close (like the 80's - laying on the bed to zipper your pants!! C-: )

it seems sturdy enough; the proportions of the backpack aspect are good and I really like the heavy duty top handle. time will tell ... my zucca case started coming apart at the seams after just 1 year. hoping this one is better reinforced

title: Many probiotic side effects are due to release and growth too early in the gut.
text:
Personal experience that may or may not be helpful.

If you have IBS, you may well have small intestinal bacterial overgrowth (SIBO). In either case, taking a probiotic that is not enteric-coated or coated for release AFTER your small intestine may make your symptoms worse because you are recolonizing your small intestine (this includes Align, and most on the market).

This product is enteric-coated, so should release after the small intestines to avoid putting bugs in the wrong place. Read product info carefully to confirm enteric-coated, since they have other products. They also have liquid versions that will dump the bugs in your small intestine.

If you're microbiome is jacked up, BIG doses of any probiotic may mess you up. I used the 12.5 billion version of this product (available elsewhere) to avoid over-doing it and it seemed OK for me, but maybe not you (see other review on backing off 25 billion). Take home message is always start small. Tread lightly.

If you react, this may be rapid growth in your small intestine with bloating.

If you react, you may be reacting to the prebiotics in the product, not the probiotic itself. Avoid prebiotics initially if very sensitive.

These bacteria may release d-lactate (brain fog).

They may make you feel worse regardless particularly if you're sensitive to histamine in food, since these strains make histamine as a byproduct of normal metabolism.

Bifidobacteria may not do this to the same degree, but these again should be engineered for release later in the gut (maybe Pearls Adult 50+ or others). Pearls Adult 50+ contain only bifidobacteria similar to those in Align. Align dumps in your small intestine.

S. boulardii is a yeast and seems to be more tolerable (to me) that may help repair and get combat bad bacteria, but it will not of itself rebalance your bacteria.

title: Pretty good rotary shaver
text:
When I was a teenager I got a Norelco as a gift and at first I got a really good shave, I really liked it over a razor. Then, I started to get such bad rashes that I finally had to give it up and went back to a razor for about 25 years.

I was too dumb to know that you had to periodically replace the heads. I just assumed that they lasted forever. Oh well.

My first new electric was a Remington, and I'll say that their adverstisements, for me, were true; It gave the closest electric shave.

The problem was my beard grows at a steep angel to the skin and electrics have a hard time getting all of the whiskers. If you missed a whisker on day one, you might get it on day two, but after that, forget it.

I went to a Braun after the Remington wore out, and it has a coarse cutter on the inside that can get those errant whiskers. It was not quite as close as the Remington, but was a lot faster because it would find the longer hairs.

This has all been to give background to the Norelco.

I've had it for over 2 months now.

It falls somewhere in between the other two. It is not quite as close as either, but very close to the Braun.

It will get those stray whiskers usually, but every 4 or 5 days, I need to use the trimmer to get ones that have gotten away.

As far as irritation goes, it borders right on the edge of a problem on my neck. The cause is obvious. The screens on this rotary are a lot rougher that a foil shaver. That is to say that you can feel the grid of depressions and holes. Be careful in pressing too hard or using repeated passes, to avoid irritation.

Because of the large surface area of the heads, I find shaving to be about 1/3 faster now, as long as I don't try to get a blade quality shave.

This razor is extremely quiet, with only a very soft whirring versus the loud buzz of any foil.

The trimmer works adequately with my only complaint that it does not lock in the up position. If you push it the wrong way, it folds back down.

The batteries last 3 to 4 weeks on a charge. If it starts to get low I just plug it in and continue shaving. To get around the lack of a low battery indicator I will charge it up before a trip so that I don't need to take the charger with me.

I consider the lack of an indicator to be a non-issue.

Overall I like the shaver a lot

title: Observations
text:
As it turns out I live only 20 miles from West Coast Shaving. I called them and said I would drop in. While I was there I picked up a bunch of different blades, a new boar brush and Mitchells wool fat shaving soap. I've been wanting to try this stuff for a long time because of the reviews. The price varies depending on where you purchase it. I bought the refill only because it would fit perfectly in an empty shaving cream container I had. Next time I will buy it in the ceramic bowl. That may be a long time from now as this soap lasts forever.

Because everyone is different and must find the best combination of razor, brush, soap and blades I will not say that this is great and that over there is terrible. But I will make an observation. A new boar brush must be broken in which takes a couple of weeks because the tips of the bristles seem too firm and rather sharp when it's first used. My observation is that not only does the lanolin soften up your skin nicely but it has helped brake in the boar bruch very quickly. The lanolin seems to soften up the brush just like it softens up your beard. This only took about three shaves and my boar brush is perfect.

I read all the reviews and saw the videos on this soap. I didn't find any problems with the soap as far a lathering. I soaked it in WARM water and took my boar brush to start to lather it up. No problem. I didn't find all the problems that other people talk about. This might be the results of the water hardness rather than the soap itself.

title: Super satisfied customer right here!

Common signs of aging such as blemishes or wrinkles become less and less visible over the course of using this. The skin just starts to look healthier. Very happy that this is all possible with this.

title: Good brush set with case
text:
This 32 brush set with pouch is pretty cool! I love the caring storage pouch it looks and feels great and it folds up really nicely. This set is exactly as pictured and described. I love doing my own make up and also do modeling for fun so having all these brushes for all my make up is great and having it all together and portable is even better! I'm not a huge fan of the bigger bushes bristles they are a little course I would rather have the Siky soft bristles that are on all the smaller brushes. So I might switch those out since they are mostly blender,foundation, and blush brushes. On of the tops on the fan brush has come apart but all the others seem pretty sturdy and well made. These brushes also do great when you wash them if you wash them the correct way for make up brushes. Even tho I had a top come off and the bristles on the big brushes were not my favorite I'd still say this is a decent quality set especially the case which is my favorite.

I did receive this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review and all opinions are my own.

title: Not a 5 Star Curler
text:
This eyelash curler is well made, and it's a good one. With that being said I will have to say that after I read all the reviews on it before I bought it, I was expecting something fabulous, and really, this is just a really good eyelash curler, nothing more. I have owned eyelash curlers for many years and my last one was a Revlon brand that I paid less than $5.00 for and for the money, it was almost as good as the Bella one. I have no regrets on buying the Bella curler, and if you're considering it, I would say go for it, it does what it's supposed to, but it won't be a glorious experience by any means as it may appear from some of the other reviews. It will pinch your eyelid if you don't grasp your lashes correctly. It will crimp your lashes if you don't grasp/squeeze properly, as any eyelash curler will do, and as some of the other reviewers stated, the curl isn't long-lasting. As I said this is a good item, just don't expect miracles.

I'm hoping the pads will be more durable than the other brands I have owned and only time will tell on that.

title: These are neat.
text:
We bought this to mix rubbing alcohol and aloe Vera for a makeshift hand sanitizer since it's so hard to find during the pandemic. These functioned perfectly and the extras were a nice touch.

Pros
1. Hasn't leaked yet.
2. Easy to fill, easy to empty, easy to clean.
3. The toothbrush covers are a nice touch.

Neutral
1. Larger than expected. Not a bad thing but definitely not pocket sized.
2. It's a bit difficult to control the flow if the stuff inside is a bit looser. In other words, it works better for lotion than it does hand sanitizer.

Cons
1. I am worried about long term durability. These are bottles technically, but they're very squishy and don't have a lot of structural integrity. Don't put heavy stuff on top of it lol. This one goes in the top of the travel bag.

Overall, these are nice little things. They are rashing squishy, hence the worry about long term durability. However, we put our hand sanitizer mix in there and it hasn't leaked out so I'm a satisfied customer.

title: The best shave soap/creme I have ever used!!!
text: The best shaving soap I have ever used. Hands down. I have been wet shaving with a safety razor for over ten years. I have been searching for the perfect shaving soap or creme for the same amount of time. I have used everything from King of Shaves, Proraso, Cremo Creme, etc, and nothing has been as comfortable as the Mitchell's Wool Fat Soap. Its an absolute joy to use. There are a few tricks to using this soap. If you want to see a good video review online, I recommend googling "Mantic,  Mitchell's Wool Fat Shaving Soap." This gentlemen provides great tips on how to get the most out of this shaving soap. For instance, for the best lather, you should use a "Boar Bristle Brush" which is stiffer than a badger brush. I have a "Super Badger Brush" which doesn't make near the lather that the stiffer, boar bristle brush makes during lathering. One last point, as other reviewers have pointed out, this soap does not have a strong scent, its just very subtle and clean. That was a plus for me. If you are looking for something with a stronger scent you may want to look elsewhere.

title: These are great stamping plates!
text:
These are great stamping plates. I've owned many plates made by different manufacturers good and bad. These are as good as anything that I have. I've tried stamping a few of the images and they all are very clear and crisp. I did these quickly on paper. The plates seem to be engraved well. It comes with two different kinds of stampers plus two scrapers. Both of the stampers work excellent. I would definitely buy these again and others made by Bluezoo. In fact I will be looking on Amazon for more plates made by them. If you are new to nail stamping one thing to know is that you must take the protective covering off of each plate, it peels right off. It takes some practice but once you have the hang of it you will love nail stamping. I would definitely recommend this set, it would be a good starter set because it comes with scrapers and stampers. I would also recommend using thicker nail polish her polish made specially for nail stamping. It seems to be easier to use than trying to use common nail polish which tends to be a little thin and often smears. I am happy with this product and would highly recommend it.

Disclaimer: I was offered this product either free or at a reduced cost in exchange for an unbiased review of the product. The opinions expressed are my own. I have no connection with the seller other than being an Amazon buyer. I hope find this review beneficial to you. Happy shopping!

title: As A Costume Wig You Can't Fault It!
text:
So I wear and review a lot of wigs and I have started breaking the review down into sections that I think will be helpful. Note this wig comes with Spider Clip (on large braid in picture), earrings, and a silver necklace (also pictured) - Not pictured - 2 clip in thin braids that I didn't use.

Color - Purple and black - obviously supposed to be a Mary Sanderson look from Hocus Pocus (although hers is a little redder) - not named as such though probably for licensing reasons.

Styling- This is a take out the bag and put on ready to go wig. There is an adjustable band at the back with minimal adjusment but it was enough to fit it securly. The style is perfect and ready to go and needed next to no effort to make it look natural.

Comfort - Fit well and would easily be wearable all day. Super light as the cap is very airy because of the ready to go style.

Overall - I LOVE this. I can't believe for a costume wig this would be so fabulous. It's instantly recognizable and the accessories were a bonus!

title: Just plain fun!
text:
I recently started wearing wigs, just to change things up and have a little fun in life. I was actually amazed at how many compliments I received while I was wearing this wig! Of course, even though the description on this is completely accurate, it's much shorter than it looks in the pictures, but I'm learning that's the case with all wigs. They must all use tiny little mannequin heads when photographing!? But now that I know this, I go by the measurements, and not the picture. The bangs were a little too long and I didn't like the way they looked with then off to the side (like in the description photo), so I cut them a bit. After that, I was very happy with it.

There's a couple small hooks in the back so you can adjust the size to fit any size head, and a netting on the inside which appears to be well made. Also, this wig is surprisingly comfortable! I wore it all day without any itching, or the wig shifting at all.

My teenage daughter laughed at me when I told her that I was gonna start wearing wigs, because I naturally have very long, very full hair, and she didn't see the point. But I explained to her, "to have fun and change things up without doing something permanent that I end up hating, but then can't change!". But once the wig arrived, she "jokingly" tried it on, and she fell in love with it too. Now she's looking forward to helping me pick out more wigs to add to my (or should I say "our") collection. I can't wait to get more colors, lengths, and styles!

title: perfect for fine hair!
text:
I have fine hair so i'm always looking for the best products that will a) not damage my hair and b) make it look good for the full day. These little hairbands do both! I have always used scrunchies to pull together outfits, but my hair stylist told me that, due to my fine hair, I really shouldn't be wearing scrunchies since they tend to pull at the hair too much and can be damaging. She recommended me these hairbands and my hair has been way healthier ever since I switched. (though, I will admit that I add a scrunchy around these hairbands every now and again, but with the hairbands doing the bulk of the work, the scrunchy just adds some flare it's a nice little secret and way for me to have it all lol)

Also these are such a cute color so i like keeping a couple around my wrist. Fun to play with for those who are anxious like me, too lol.

And I like that it doesn't leave a crease in my hair, too. My hair, since it's so fine, tends to be ruined anytime i put it in an updo since it makes that weird bump/ crease lol. so i can wear my hair in an updo with these babies during the day and then still put it down at night without having to worry about a straightener to hide any bumps!

definitely a good buy for anyone looking to have a healthier updo!!

title: So far so awesome: Day 1
text:
Notes out of the box;
* Very nice, low profile footprint design.
* 7 inches long, 5 inches at widest part of the oval, 2.5 inches tall.
* Fits up to .75 oz polishes with all shaped bottle necks (bam! take that iZen Pro!)
* Silver plastic, no idea on solvent resistance (yet).
* Uses both AC adapter or batteries for cordless operation.
* 5600 shakes per minute promises the box and spec detail.
* 3 detachable, adjustable rubber securing straps.
* 1 on/off button, with a lighter pressure 'pulse on' feature.

Operation:
* Cinch to strap on.
* Pretty impressive agitation for one so wee.
* Noise level mild especially in comparison to my incessant bottle agitation whining. "Ow! My arms! Blah, blah, blah." I'd be more specific, but I was operating it right on a stainless steel counter to put it through the noise test. It'd be unladylike to compare it to a 'personal massager' out loud wouldn't it? Did I just type that? Oopsy, my bad. Quieter over a mat, silicone, towel or cork tile by miles. I did that too.

Result:
Before had separation, some streaky clearness and clinging pigment near the neck. I gave it my regular human powered roll/tap/roll for the following 'before shaker' application demo. (See posted pics)

Left, regular human agitation. 5 freaky coats and I had to wrangle dual directions and mucho smoothing in application. I tried really hard to give it more care actually because I wanted to be ungenerous to ForPro. But, human agitation was a streaky, bubbled shrink-back, difficult, wrinkling mess at every application and cure. Just like last time I dared to be decadent. I purposefully used a problem child color of an otherwise fabulous gel polish line. Plus, I left the cleansing paper towel wipes in the photos to demonstrate the horrid vs acceptable cure difference. Like eww! That's dangerous. It even wiped off in spots!

Right, ForPro shaken. 3 coats. Application was easy and like normally behaved other vampy, dark, well agitated gel polishes. Say what? I thought I'd see some improvement, but I didn't really expect this great of one. The color is wearable and I like it now.

All things are equal in my test, except I was more careful in application of the human shaken swatch, which has 2 more coats for a subpar, unusable result. I had to, it was having a tantrum in streaky pants before ForPro.

Other notes:
ForPro requires a phillips head screwdriver to open the 4 AA battery housing. Probably because it shakes so much? Still, a nice cordless option to the AC adapter also included. Yay that. *Batteries not included.

And, I think any warranty would be online via ForPro website only? This didn't come with instructions or warranty card in the box. Mostly a no-brainer plug and play.

It's unscathed by alcohol meaning any gel polish mishaps can wipe right off. I blotted the underbelly to remove dust lightly.

Clear rubber feet stay put while leaving no marks. That's awesome. Especially compared to Robarts that jiggle off tables and sound like landscaping machines.

I like ForPro so far. I think it saves product and my shake stress physiology. Prettier result in fewer, easier coats :).

It did a good job on both a chunky glitter and a streak-prone frost in a conventional lacquer brand. I did shake them once to break surface tension first though. I also rotated the bottles and let them shake twice. The beads were rolling free in both and additive distribution was even and good after. Thanks ForPro! Please last an eon now.

title: Great for touch-ups
text:
After years of electrolysis, every once in a while, I will get a stray hair that pops up. I don't really feel like driving the hour to the nearest electrologist and buying a whole session for just one hair. For this purpose, this little machine is excellent.
If you've never had electrolysis, I wouldn't recommend it. Knowing what to expect this to feel like has helped me use it effectively.
Looking to remove all the hair on your legs? Unless you want to take this on as a full time job, I wouldn't recommend it. It is very slow.
The included tweezers are next to useless. You probably have some at home that work. Use those.
Since I am not professionally trained, and this is not a professional machine, I find it is easiest to use on thick hairs. I have a very difficult time trying to use the probe on thin hairs.
Like the other reviewers, I find the beep annoying. I'm sure there could have been a better way to design this to indicate when the current is on. But at least I know it's working.
At this price, for touch-ups, it is perfect. Keep your expectations in line with the price.

For the first time in my life I didn't have to spend 30 minutes of my day picking through my hair with a comb/brush, in front of a mirror, and ripping out multiple rats. title: gentle cleansing, lots of suds, leaves skin clean but not parched, great for travel
text:
Individually wrapped cleansing cloths that are way better than any other I have used in the past. The material has a good strong feel, and it is large enough that it nearly feels like a washcloth. I can really get my hand on it. The packets easily tear open. The cloth, once wet becomes crazy sudsy. It has a barely there scent. I really have to sniff to be able to detect anything. Very fresh. The lather is gentle and non drying. I was surprised that my face was not feeling parched or taut afterward.

Not only for face, but it makes for a decent cleansing cloth to have a "bird bath" at the sink. Just wipe down everywhere you need to, rinse the cloth, rinse your body, rinse the cloth again, ring it out, use it to pat down the body.. and away you go.

I think one could get away with using half a cloth easily for just face washing as there is plenty of cleaning agent in it.

Overall it is a lovely product. I think it is perfectly packaged for occasional use, or for on the go/ travel. If I were to use it all the time, I would prefer something with less packaging. Maybe they have that... I didn't look.

But for travel: definite yes. I would go so far as to use one in the shower to take care of face and entire body. The cleanser is so gentle, which is a huge draw for me and my dry/rosacea skin.

title: Taming mustaches and planting trees!
text:
My husband has a ridiculously oversized, extremely thick and wiry handlebar mustache. He started growing it several years ago as sort of a joke / challenge and it somehow became his signature look and now he can never shave it off. He's tried lots of waxes to defy gravity and hold his hair in his desired shape. He really likes Death Grip and the old-fashioned Clubman soft wax that comes tinted in a tube.

This Tree-Topped Mustache Wax is solid in a little tin like the Death Grip, but it's slightly softer - soft enough you can dig some out of the tin and apply it just with the warmth of your hands without having to warm up the whole tin in hot water or with a blow dryer. But since it's a little softer instead of a rock-hard wax, it's also has a little less hold. I'd call it a medium-firm hold.

Absolutely love that it's simple, clean, and UNSCENTED! And that they're planting trees for each tin sold! And they even give you the recipe if you want to whip some up and refill your tin yourself. I absolutely will be ordering from this company again!

title: Used for more than a dozen times. Here’s my tips and review.
text:
Okay so I have used this product more than a dozen times more than a year. I love it. Works great for my eye brows. Here is what I have learned:
1. It can stain your counters/sink. Put a towel or rag down. If you do get it on your counters clean immediately. Soft scrub works well!
2. It doesn’t stain your skin. Don’t worry about trying to be neat about it and being detailed to your eye brow. Just slap that crap all over and set your phone timer for however long you want for how dark you want (I prefer 3 mins for a dark brown)
3. Set up. Mixing in that little bowl can be messy. I like to use a Q tip to slowly mix it. This way it does not get everywhere on my brush and I make sure I don’t leave any unmixed powder on my brush when I apply to my face.
4. Clean up. Cleaning that bowl again don’t want to get on the counters when you have extra left in the bowl. I use a baby wipe to get the extra out before rinsing the bowl and brush. This way I make sure my counters stay unstained!

title: My Favorite Beauty Helper
text:
OMG !!!!!!! I have been cruising Amazon for this exact item for quite some time and was thrilled to see it for an excellent price. I had been to retail and beauty supply stores everywhere I went, only to be told I could not order just the unit. I had already purchased extra rollers with my original unit. My Caruso Steam Unit gave up the after some major miles, through many hair styles.
I have very thick, straight American Indian hair that was prematurely gray hair before I could graduate high school. I purchased the Caruso Steam set and many extra curlers years ago when it first came on the market. I had tried various kinds of steam roller sets that were difficult to wrap my straight hair around. They were pokey and heavy with painful steel pins to hold rollers in my hair; only for the curls to fall out almost immediately. I tried ceramic, adjustable heat settings, and many different curling irons and dryers that was new on the market. I combined hair gels or mousse with them only to have curls and waves fall out within minutes. I went to beauty school (lol) after high school. Really not my thing
The new unit I received is not round like this picture. It is kidney shaped.
The old version I had to add a small amount of salt to the water.
The newer version has a slider switch for OFF, STEAM and IONIC STEAM.
I love, love, love the Caruso Steam for my hair.
I used 6-8 curlers for my whole head.
Literally seconds to put each in and about the time I get them all in it is time to take them out. Just like my old Caruso Steam unit (without the salt).
My curls last three days minimum.
I DO NOT use hair products on my hair ever.
Curls are soft and natural feeling.
Caruso Steamer does not damage hair. Stopped perms many years ago after finding this steamer. This amazing steamer adds shine to my curls. I can run my fingers through my hair as much as I want.
There is never those bent ends of hair, curls just fall, I shake my head, move a curl or two with my fingers and I am out the door.
I have missed this little time saver and am continually complimented on my hair and how much younger I look.
I hope this helps someone, lets me know how you use your Caruso Steamer
